dimension_evaluation.what_novel: Reflexion does not claim or empirically support that structured and episodic memories are unified under one overarching principle nor that their combination subsumes prior methods; this explicit unification is not present in the paper and thus is a novel claim relative to Reflexion.

unification.paper_title: Learning Knowledge Graph-based World Models of Textual Environments

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: The paper explicitly unifies two tasks—knowledge-graph state prediction and valid-action generation—into a single multi-task transformer architecture and reframes both outputs as Set-of-Sequences, showing they are mutually informative for world modeling.
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: The theory asserts a unification at the level of memory modalities: that structured and episodic memories should be treated as complementary components of a single hybrid-memory principle for LLM agents, with joint retrieval and integration into working memory producing systematic benefits.
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: The paper does not claim or empirically evaluate unifying structured and episodic memory modalities; its unification is between prediction tasks, not memory systems. The proposed memory-modality unification is therefore a new conceptual unification relative to this work.

unification.paper_title: Language Models are Few-Shot Butlers

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: The paper explicitly unifies imitation learning and reinforcement-style self-improvement by optimizing the same action-modeling objective on both expert demonstrations and successful agent-generated trajectories (iterated action modeling), connecting two training paradigms within one framework.
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: The theory asserts a different unification: that structured-memory approaches (e.g., knowledge graphs/semantic state) and episodic-memory approaches (trajectory/reflective memory) can be seen as instances of a single underlying architectural principle whose purposeful integration yields synergistic agent benefits.
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: The paper does not perform a conceptual or empirical unification of structured and episodic memory modalities; its unification is orthogonal (imitation + self-imitation). Therefore the proposed unification of memory modalities is not present in the paper and is novel relative to it.

unification.paper_title: Thespian: Multi-Character Text Role-Playing Game Agents

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: This paper unifies several technical ideas for multi-behavior text-RL: soft prompt conditioning, multi-headed actor/critic outputs (one head per character), and an attention-based fusion module for few-shot composition, built on a KG-A2C structured-memory base.
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: The theory states an explicit unification principle that structured (relational) and episodic (trajectory/reflection) memories are complementary components of a single hybrid-memory framework whose joint use explains and improves agent behavior in text games.
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: The paper does not claim or demonstrate that structured and episodic memories are instances of one unified hybrid principle nor show cross-cutting instances from prior work that this hybrid principle subsumes, so the unification claim is not present in the paper.

unification.paper_title: diff History for Neural Language Agents

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: The paper situates diff history among prior abstraction techniques (e.g., Scratchpad, Chain-of-Thought) and shows that a task-agnostic, procedural textual abstraction can improve LM agent learning and generalization, implicitly relating representation compression and relational abstraction across environments.
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: The theory asserts an explicit unification claim: that structured (knowledge/semantic graphs) and episodic (trajectory) memories are complementary modalities whose principled combination is a single underlying mechanism driving robust, generalizable LLM-agent performance.
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: The paper does not assert or demonstrate this explicit unification across diverse memory formalisms; it evaluates one specific structured-episodic encoding (diff deltas) but does not show that graph-style structured memories and episodic traces are instances of one unifying principle or that they jointly implement the same mechanism. The unification claim is therefore not present in the paper.

generalization_scope_expansion.paper_title: AriGraph: Learning Knowledge Graph World Models with Episodic Memory for LLM Agents

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: The paper evaluates AriGraph across multiple interactive text-game families (Treasure Hunt, Cleaning, Cooking), a constrained NetHack setting, and adapts the architecture to static multi-hop QA benchmarks (MuSiQue, HotpotQA), reporting robust performance and scaling behavior.
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: The theory states the hybrid-memory advantage holds broadly for LLM agents in partially observable, combinatorial-action, long-horizon text-game domains (e.g., TextWorld, NetHack) and in tasks requiring multi-step planning and multi-hop reasoning.
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: The paper explicitly demonstrates cross-domain applicability and provides evidence that the approach generalizes beyond a single game family (interactive games and QA), so the scope expansion claimed by the theory is supported and already present in the paper.

generalization_scope_expansion.paper_title: Knowledge-enhanced Agents for Interactive Text Games

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: Previous KG- and memory-based techniques were evaluated in particular agents or environments; large-scale multi-task empirical evaluation across diverse ScienceWorld tasks and both RL and LM agent classes was lacking.
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: The paper extends evaluation of affordance and episodic-memory injection to ten heterogeneous ScienceWorld tasks and to both RL (online) and LM (offline) paradigms, showing where injections transfer and where they fail.
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: This is an empirical expansion of scope (more tasks, multiple agent families, multiple injection strategies) rather than a theoretical generalization; it documents the transferability and limits of these injections across settings.

generalization_scope_expansion.paper_title: A Survey on Large Language Model-Based Game Agents

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: The survey maps memory and reasoning requirements across multiple game genres (action, adventure, sandbox, strategy, simulation, role-playing) and discusses how memory designs address genre-specific demands (e.g., stateful world modeling in adventure games, low-latency trade-offs in action games).
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: The theory claims that hybrid structured+episodic memory integration produces robust, sample-efficient, and generalizable gains specifically for LLM agents in partially observable, combinatorial-action, long-horizon text-game-like domains (e.g., TextWorld, NetHack, other text-based tasks).
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: The paper supplies cross-domain groundwork and genre mappings but does not empirically validate that hybrid memory integration consistently generalizes across the full scope of these domains; the explicit scope-expansion claim (uniform hybrid benefit across specified game classes) is therefore not established in the survey and remains a derived generalization.

generalization_scope_expansion.paper_title: Reasoning, Memorization, and Fine-Tuning Language Models for Non-Cooperative Games

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: The paper validates its method on a newly defined non-cooperative game (Goedendag) across two board sizes (4x4, 10x10), reports cross-board fine-tuning gains, and argues the approach could extend to other game types; empirical claims are constrained to this game and configuration.
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: The theory claims a broader scope: hybrid structured+episodic memory architectures will improve robustness, sample efficiency, and generalization for LLM agents across a wide class of text-based games with partial observability, combinatorial actions, and long-horizon planning (e.g., TextWorld, NetHack).
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: Extending the paper's demonstrated advantages of compact representations and task decomposition to the broad class of environments and to hybrid memory architectures is not tested here; while the extension is a plausible generalization, it is not demonstrated and remains an unstated derivation from the paper's limited empirical scope.

generalization_scope_expansion.paper_title: Reflexion: Language Agents with Verbal Reinforcement Learning

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: Reflexion demonstrates cross-task generalization of episodic verbal reinforcement across decision-making, reasoning, and programming tasks and evaluates variations in memory size, evaluator types, and base model strength.
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: The theory extends the claim to a broader scope, asserting that combining structured semantic/graph memories with episodic reflections yields robustness, sample efficiency, and generalization in harder, partial-observability, combinatorial-action domains (e.g., NetHack-like environments) beyond the textual episodic traces tested.
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: Extending Reflexion's episodic-memory results to explicit structured+episodic benefits across broader domains is not demonstrated in the paper; while plausible from background literature, this extension is not tested here and is therefore a derivable-but-unstated generalization relative to the paper.

generalization_scope_expansion.paper_title: Learning Knowledge Graph-based World Models of Textual Environments

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: The paper's domain is LLM/world-modeling in text-based games with partial observability and large action vocabularies; experiments are on JerichoWorld games and the claims are about improved KG and action prediction generalization to never-before-seen textual worlds within that benchmark.
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: The theory extends the claim to a broader scope: hybrid structured+episodic memory will increase robustness, sample efficiency, and generalization of LLM agents across text-game domains (including more complex environments like NetHack and long-horizon planning tasks) compared to single-memory or unstructured-history approaches.
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: While the paper shows structured-memory benefits within JerichoWorld prediction tasks, it does not evaluate hybrid memory or downstream gameplay performance or larger/different environments; the theory's broader scope (gameplay-level normalized performance, NetHack-scale environments, and sample efficiency claims) is not established by this paper.

generalization_scope_expansion.paper_title: Generative Agents: Interactive Simulacra of Human Behavior

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: The authors demonstrate the architecture in a visual/simulated Smallville sandbox with 25 agents and argue qualitatively that the memory+reflection+planning pattern could transfer to other interactive settings; they discuss but do not empirically test extensions to other domains.
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: The Hybrid Memory Synergy Law explicitly scopes the claim to LLM agents in text-based games with partial observability, combinatorial action spaces, and complex state transitions (e.g., TextWorld, NetHack), asserting that a hybrid structured+episodic memory yields robustness, sample efficiency, and generalization in those domains.
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: Applying the paper's Smallville results to complex text-adventure / partially observable combinatorial-action domains and claiming improved sample efficiency and generalization is a substantive scope expansion not evaluated in this paper; the paper does not provide experiments or metrics in those target domains to support that generalization.

generalization_scope_expansion.paper_title: Monte Carlo Planning with Large Language Model for Text-Based Game Agents

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: The paper demonstrates MC-DML's effectiveness across a diverse set of nine Jericho games, including long-horizon and sparse-reward tasks, indicating generalization across multiple games within the text-game POMDP domain.
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: It proposes that dynamic memory-guided LLM priors can improve initial planning across varied, hard textual games without a planning-then-learning cycle, and provides empirical support for that claim within the Jericho benchmark.
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: The theory extends this scope further (e.g., TextWorld, NetHack, and claims about robustness to partial observability and combinatorial action spaces when combining structured and episodic memory); those broader domain generalizations and the structured+episodic scope expansion are not evaluated in the paper and therefore remain untested here.

generalization_scope_expansion.paper_title: diff History for Neural Language Agents

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: Empirically, the paper demonstrates diff history benefits in two procedurally-generated text environments of different dimensionality (BabyAI-Text and NetHack), reports scaling with more data in NetHack, and characterizes when diff compresses versus expands token counts.
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: The theory generalizes to a broader architectural claim: hybrid structured+episodic memory systems will improve robustness, sample efficiency, and generalization for LLM agents across partially-observable, combinatorial, long-horizon text-game domains (e.g., TextWorld, NetHack) and will outperform agents with only one memory type or naive full-history retrieval.
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: Extending the paper's result (that a specific structured episodic encoding helps) to the broader architectural generalization that arbitrary hybrid structured+episodic memory systems will systematically outperform single-memory alternatives is not tested; the paper's experiments make the hybrid hypothesis plausible but do not establish it across memory architectures or domains.

generalization_scope_expansion.paper_title: Evaluating and Enhancing LLMs Agent based on Theory of Mind in Guandan (搜蛋): A Multi-Player Cooperative Game under Imperfect Information

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: The paper demonstrates that prompting-based Theory-of-Mind (zero/first/second-order) planning and an external RL-based action recommender enable off-the-shelf LLMs to operate in a complex, non-English, multi-player imperfect-information card game (Guandan). It shows consistent performance improvements across multiple LLM families and model sizes when ToM planning and the external tool are applied. The work explicitly applies modular LLM-agent components (State Interpreter, ToM Planner, Plan Evaluator) to a realistic card-game domain.
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: The theory statement generalizes the benefit of hybrid structured+episodic memory to text-game LLM agents and claims improved robustness, sample efficiency and generalization in partial observability and long-horizon planning tasks. It scopes that claim to environments like TextWorld, NetHack and text-based tasks requiring multi-step planning and navigation.
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: The paper does generalize prior LLM planning/ToM work to a new, culturally-specific, multi-agent imperfect-information card game and to non-English settings; however it does not implement or evaluate hybrid structured+episodic memory. Therefore the paper provides partial, domain-level generalization evidence for LLM-agent augmentation (ToM + external tool) but not for the specific hybrid-memory claim.

generalization_scope_expansion.paper_title: RAP: Retrieval-Augmented Planning with Contextual Memory for Multimodal LLM Agents

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: RAP generalizes retrieval-augmented episodic memory beyond pure text tasks into multimodal embodied benchmarks and across multiple LLM/VLM backbones, and demonstrates cross-model transfer of stored experiences (memories built with one model can benefit another).
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: The theory claims a broader generalization: that hybrid structured+episodic memory yields robust, sample-efficient, and generalizable performance specifically in partially observable, combinatorial-action, long-horizon text-game domains (e.g., TextWorld, NetHack) and other complex sequential decision settings.
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: The paper validates multimodal and textual generalization for episodic retrieval but does not test the additional axis of generalization to hybrid structured+episodic systems nor to the more extreme, combinatorial domains named; that further scope-expansion is not demonstrated in the paper.

generalization_scope_expansion.paper_title: Pre-trained Language Models as Prior Knowledge for Playing Text-based Games

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: The paper demonstrates LM-augmentation across 14 Jericho games, reports improved sample efficiency and limited transfer (Zork1→Zork3), and emphasizes applicability to IF/text-based game benchmarks that are partially observable with combinatorial action spaces.
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: The theory expands scope by claiming the hybrid structured+episodic memory principle will produce robust, generalizable gains for LLM agents across a broader class of challenging text environments (e.g., TextWorld, NetHack, long-horizon planning and navigation tasks) beyond LM-only augmentation.
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: The paper's experiments show LM benefits within Jericho, but they do not test hybrid structured+episodic architectures nor domains like NetHack; extending the claim to those broader settings is an extrapolation not validated in the paper and therefore constitutes a novel scope expansion.

generalization_scope_expansion.paper_title: Large Language Models Are Neurosymbolic Reasoners

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: The paper demonstrates that zero-shot LLM agents with symbolic modules generalize across four symbolic tasks (Arithmetic, MapReader, Sorting, TWC) within TextWorld-derived benchmarks and across two model families (GPT-3.5, GPT-4), showing the prompting+module approach transfers within this benchmark suite.
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: The Hybrid Memory Synergy Law extends scope by claiming that hybrid structured+episodic memory augmentation will improve robustness, sample efficiency, and generalization for LLM agents broadly in partially observable, combinatorial, long-horizon text-game domains (e.g., TextWorld, NetHack, and other complex text-based tasks).
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: The paper does not evaluate hybrid memory across these broader domains or scales; its experiments are limited to symbolic-module tool use on TextWorldExpress tasks, so the broader scope claim (other environments, more complex partial observability or combinatorial-action domains) is not supported or tested here.

generalization_scope_expansion.paper_title: Graph Constrained Reinforcement Learning for Natural Language Action Spaces

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: The paper demonstrates cross-game generality of the KG+template approach by evaluating on 28 diverse human-authored IF games and describes relaxing graph-extraction rules to improve applicability across genres.
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: The theory expands scope to (a) explicitly hybrid structured+episodic memory systems, (b) application to LLM-based decision architectures (rather than the GRU/GAT policies used here), and (c) broader domains requiring longer-horizon planning (e.g., NetHack, TextWorld variants).
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: Extending the KG+template insights to include episodic-memory integration and to LLM-driven agents and additional domains is not demonstrated in the paper; these scope expansions are additional claims beyond the paper's experiments and thus novel relative to it.

generalization_scope_expansion.paper_title: Deciphering Digital Detectives: Understanding LLM Behaviors and Capabilities in Multi-Agent Mystery Games

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: The paper demonstrates benefits of MR+SR+SV in one domain (Chinese Jubensha multi-agent detective games) and reports limitations and domain-specific caveats (language specificity, stochasticity, cost), but provides no cross-environment empirical validation.
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: The theory generalizes the claim to a broader class of LLM agent text-game domains (e.g., TextWorld, NetHack) and to settings with partial observability, combinatorial actions, and long-horizon planning, asserting superiority of a hybrid structured+episodic memory over single/unstructured memories.
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: Extending the paper's single-domain experimental findings to a broad cross-environment claim about structured+episodic hybrids is not demonstrated or analyzed in the paper and therefore represents a novel generalization beyond the presented evidence.

generalization_scope_expansion.paper_title: MemInsight: Autonomous Memory Augmentation for LLM Agents

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: MemInsight's experiments and empirical claims are confined to text-dialogue tasks (LLM-REDIAL, LoCoMo) — QA, recommendation, and summarization — and the authors note the implementation is text-only and could be extended to multimodal inputs in future work.
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: The theory extends the claimed benefits to a broader and more challenging domain: LLM agents in text-based games with partial observability, combinatorial action spaces, and long-horizon planning (e.g., TextWorld, NetHack), asserting improved robustness and sample efficiency there.
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: This cross-domain extrapolation (from dialogue benchmarks to complex interactive text-game environments) is a substantive scope expansion not supported by experiments or analysis in the paper, so it represents a genuine generalization beyond the presented evidence.

generalization_scope_expansion.paper_title: Positive Experience Reflection for Agents in Interactive Text Environments

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: The paper demonstrates Sweet&Sour on ScienceWorld tasks and across three LLM sizes, and claims applicability to interactive text environments that use self-reflection and managed memory.
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: The theory asserts a broader scope: hybrid structured+episodic memory improves robustness, sample efficiency, and generalization across partially observable, combinatorial-action text-game domains (e.g., TextWorld, NetHack) and outperforms single-memory or unstructured full-history retrieval.
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: While the paper generalizes episodic/managed-reflection benefits within one benchmark and multiple LLMs, it provides no experiments or analysis showing that adding structured relational memory yields the claimed cross-domain/generalization benefits; that expanded hybrid-scope claim is not supported here.

generalization_scope_expansion.paper_title: Behavior Cloned Transformers are Neurosymbolic Reasoners

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: Empirically, the paper shows that action-injection generalizes across four distinct text-game tasks and is implementable with different symbolic modules and a behavior-cloned T5 model, but experiments are restricted to gold-trajectory supervision, the valid-action aid, and specific benchmarks.
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: The theory claims broader scope: that hybrid structured+episodic memory integration improves robustness, sample efficiency, and generalization for LLM agents across text games with partial observability, combinatorial action spaces, and long-horizon planning (e.g., TextWorld, NetHack), surpassing single-memory or unstructured-history approaches.
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: Although the paper supports that structured symbolic augmentation helps in several benchmarks, it does not evaluate episodic-memory mechanisms, principled joint-retrieval policies, or varied LLM architectures and environments at the scale claimed by the theory, so that scope expansion is not established here.

generalization_scope_expansion.paper_title: A Mixture-of-Experts Approach to Few-Shot Task Transfer in Open-Ended Text Worlds

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: The paper demonstrates results in a LIGHT-derived open-ended text-role environment using KG-A2C experts and evaluates blended/partial-blend role transfer; it does not test LLM-based agents, NetHack, TextWorld, or explicit episodic-memory modules.
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: The theory claims the hybrid structured+episodic memory principle generalizes to LLM-based agents across partially-observable, combinatorial-action text games and yields superior robustness, sample efficiency, and generalization over single-memory or naive full-history retrieval approaches.
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: Extending the paper's MoE+KG results to LLM-based agents and to a broad class of text-game domains (and to explicit episodic memory mechanisms) is not demonstrated or evaluated here; that scope expansion is a substantive, novel claim beyond the paper's experiments.

generalization_scope_expansion.paper_title: Language Models are Few-Shot Butlers

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: Empirically, the paper demonstrates in-distribution and out-of-distribution generalization within ALFWorld, including robustness to human-annotated goals, and quantifies sample-efficiency gains from iterated action modeling; experiments are scoped to ALFWorld and use sliding context windows and specific decoding/hyperparameter settings.
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: The theory statement explicitly claims broader scope: that hybrid structured+episodic memory benefits apply to LLM agents across partially-observable, combinatorial, long-horizon text games (e.g., TextWorld, NetHack) and enable overcoming partial observability and long-horizon credit assignment more generally.
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: Cross-environment generalization and the claim that hybrid memory architectures deliver similar benefits across diverse text-game domains (and scale beyond ALFWorld) are not demonstrated in the paper, nor is structured memory evaluated; thus the scope expansion in the theory is not supported by this work.

generalization_scope_expansion.paper_title: Thespian: Multi-Character Text Role-Playing Game Agents

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: The paper's experiments and claims are scoped to the LIGHT text role-playing environment and variants of that map, and the method is evaluated for multi-character learning and few-shot adaptation within that single environment family.
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: The theory asserts broader applicability: that hybrid structured+episodic memory improves robustness, sample efficiency, and generalization for LLM-based agents across a range of text-game domains (e.g., TextWorld, NetHack) and in tasks requiring multi-step planning and long-horizon instruction following.
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: Those broader domain and modality generalizations are not evaluated in the paper; extending the empirical claim to other text-game platforms and to explicit episodic memory components is a novel expansion beyond this work.

generalization_scope_expansion.paper_title: Keep CALM and Explore: Language Models for Action Generation in Text-based Games

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: This work demonstrates that a single LM (CALM) trained on diverse human transcripts generalizes to unseen Jericho games and that the LM+DRRN pipeline can be applied across many man-made text games with partial observability and large action spaces; the paper also explores sensitivity to k and pretraining.
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: The Hybrid Memory Synergy Law explicitly generalizes the claim to a broader class of LLM agents and tasks: that hybrid structured+episodic memory integration improves robustness, sample efficiency, and generalization across text-based games requiring multi-step planning, navigation, and long-horizon instruction following (e.g., TextWorld, NetHack).
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: Extending the empirical claim from LM-only candidate generation (paper) to a broad cross-methods claim about hybrid memory systems and their advantages across a wider set of environments and failure modes is not supported or addressed in the paper; the hybrid scope expansion goes beyond the experiments and domains evaluated.

generalization_scope_expansion.paper_title: Learning Dynamic Belief Graphs to Generalize on Text-Based Games

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: The paper demonstrates distributional generalization within TextWorld: an agent trained on a set of generated games generalizes to unseen games across multiple difficulty levels and mixed-level testing, showing structured belief graphs improve cross-instance generalization in this environment family.
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: The theory claims a broader scope: that hybrid structured+episodic memory will enable robust, sample-efficient, and generalizable performance for LLM-based agents across many text-game domains (e.g., TextWorld and NetHack) and more generally in long-horizon, partially observable language-mediated tasks.
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: Extending the TextWorld-specific empirical result to other environments and to a general statement about hybrid memory for LLM agents is not supported by the paper’s experiments; that scope expansion is a substantive, new claim beyond the paper’s demonstrated domain.

constraint_limitation.paper_title: AriGraph: Learning Knowledge Graph World Models with Episodic Memory for LLM Agents

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: The paper reports limitations and task-specific effects: hybrid memory yields little advantage for trivial short-horizon tasks; episodic vs semantic contributions differ by task (episodic important for Cooking), and noisy/unreliable triplet extraction or low-quality LLM backbones degrade graph growth/update quality and performance.
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: The theory lists special cases: minimal benefit when short-term context suffices and disrupted gains if memory construction is highly unreliable; these are framed as necessary caveats to applicability.
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: Those constraints and failure modes are explicitly discussed, investigated via ablations and analysis (graph growth vs LLM quality, AriGraph w/o episodic), and thus the limitations in the theory are already documented in the paper.

constraint_limitation.paper_title: A Survey on Large Language Model-Based Game Agents

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: The survey explicitly discusses limitations and failure modes: working-memory capacity and inference-latency trade-offs, unreliable extraction/noisy triplet construction degrading downstream retrieval, and cases where short-term context suffices so long-term memory yields little benefit.
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: The theory includes matching scoped exceptions (little benefit for tasks solvable with short-term context; degraded gains when memory construction is unreliable), framing when hybrid memory is unlikely to help.
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: These constraints and special cases are already present and discussed in the survey, so the limitation content in the theory is already explicit and established by the reviewed literature.

constraint_limitation.paper_title: MemInsight: Autonomous Memory Augmentation for LLM Agents

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: The paper explicitly reports limitations: augmentations can be overly generic in ambiguous contexts, performance depends on the backbone LLM quality, occasional failed attribute extraction (small fraction), and some metrics do not uniformly improve; it also states current work is limited to text interactions.
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: The theory lists special cases where hybrid memory gives little benefit (short-term context/trivial mappings) and where unreliable memory construction disrupts benefits (e.g., poor triplet extraction).
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: These constraints closely match the paper's documented limitations (dependence on LLM quality, unreliable augmentations reducing benefit and limited benefit for trivial tasks), so the theory's constraints are already acknowledged in the paper.

constraint_limitation.paper_title: Reflexion: Language Agents with Verbal Reinforcement Learning

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: The paper explicitly documents limitations: Reflexion's effectiveness depends on evaluator/self-evaluation quality (and test generation in programming), uses a bounded sliding-window memory that limits long-horizon persistence, can get stuck in local minima (WebShop failure), and suffers if internal tests are flaky or incorrect.
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: The theory likewise acknowledges limits: limited benefit for tasks solvable from short-term context and degraded or disrupted benefits when structured-memory construction (e.g., noisy triplet extraction) is unreliable.
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: These constraint/limitation claims substantially overlap with and are already explicit in the paper (dependence on evaluator quality, memory-window limits, local-minima failure modes), so the paper already establishes the relevant constraints and the theory's similar caveats are not novel with respect to Reflexion.

constraint_limitation.paper_title: Learning Dynamic Belief Graphs to Generalize on Text-Based Games

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: The paper explicitly documents limitations and failure modes: learned continuous belief graphs can be dense/noisy and include non-interpretable features; discrete graph updaters are prone to error accumulation; GATA underperforms an agent with full ground-truth graphs (upper bound); and simple short-horizon tasks yield little benefit from structured representations.
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: The theory’s listed constraints (no benefit for trivial short-context games; unreliable memory-construction reduces or disrupts gains) closely match the paper’s observed failure regimes and cautions about noisy or brittle graph construction.
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: These constraints are explicitly observed and discussed in the paper and therefore are not novel relative to the paper.

constraint_limitation.paper_title: Large Language Models Are Neurosymbolic Reasoners

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: The paper explicitly documents practical limitations: constrained prompts affect performance, agents forget navigation routes (increasing steps), limited internal/context memory hinders sorting logic, and agents remain error-prone and uncertain in some tasks; it suggests more detailed prompts or improved modules could mitigate these issues.
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: The theory lists special-case constraints: hybrid-memory benefits may be negligible for tasks solvable from short-term context, and benefits diminish if memory construction (e.g., triplet extraction) is highly unreliable.
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: There is overlap between the paper's documented failure modes (forgetting, limited memory capacity, task-dependent prompt efficacy) and the theory's caveats; however, the paper does not analyze these as formal necessary conditions for a hybrid-memory approach nor experimentally probe reliability thresholds for memory construction. Thus the constraint-related observations exist in the paper but only peripherally relative to the hybrid-memory claim.

constraint_limitation.paper_title: Graph Constrained Reinforcement Learning for Natural Language Action Spaces

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: The paper documents practical limitations and necessary components: access to valid-action supervision (Jericho), the template action space (vs. full vocabulary decoding), and robust triplet extraction (OpenIE plus custom rules) are important for performance; ablations show removing these degrades results.
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: The theory enumerates special cases where hybrid-memory benefits are reduced: (1) tasks solvable from short-term context may show little benefit and (2) highly unreliable memory construction (e.g., noisy triplet extraction) can disrupt hybrid-memory gains.
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: These cautions echo limitations already discussed or implied in the paper (need for valid-action signals, template necessity, OpenIE extraction issues); the theory's explicit articulation of unreliable memory-construction as a failure mode is consistent with the paper and represents a peripheral formalization rather than a new constraint discovery.

constraint_limitation.paper_title: Positive Experience Reflection for Agents in Interactive Text Environments

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: The paper explicitly notes limitations: results are from a single environment (ScienceWorld), reflection gives little benefit on trivial/short-horizon tasks, and effectiveness depends on model capacity (smaller LLMs benefit differently).
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: The theory lists constraints including little benefit for tasks solvable with short-term context and reduced/negative effects when structured-memory construction (e.g., unreliable triplet extraction) is noisy or unreliable.
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: The short-task and model-size sensitivity constraints are present and discussed in the paper; however the specific constraint about failures arising from unreliable structured-memory construction is not evaluated or discussed and therefore is novel relative to this paper (a peripheral but untested caveat).

constraint_limitation.paper_title: Behavior Cloned Transformers are Neurosymbolic Reasoners

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: The paper explicitly documents key limitations that constrain applicability: privileged access to exhaustive valid-action lists (valid-action aid), reliance on gold trajectories for behavior cloning, and the DRRN's failure under their setup; it also notes action-space growth when many module actions are injected and dependence on pretrained model priors for some tasks.
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: The theory lists special-case constraints (little benefit for trivially solvable short-horizon games and reduced gains when structured-memory extraction is highly unreliable) that bound when hybrid-memory integration will help.
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: The paper contains overlapping but different constraints; it does not analyze or quantify failure modes arising from noisy structured-memory construction or delineate the boundary between short-term-context tasks and those requiring hybrid memory, so the theory's specific constraints are only partially anticipated by the paper and are not emphasized as contributions.

constraint_limitation.paper_title: Reasoning, Memorization, and Fine-Tuning Language Models for Non-Cooperative Games

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: The paper reports concrete limitations: higher token/inference cost from using four agents and back-and-forth communication, compute limits on fine-tuning experiments, reliance on replacing invalid actions with random valid ones during evaluation, and clear performance degradation when summarization or area-selection are removed.
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: The theory enumerates failure regimes and necessary conditions: trivial short-horizon games may show little benefit from hybrid memory, and unreliable structured-memory construction (e.g., noisy triplet extraction) can reduce or disrupt gains from hybrid architectures.
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: The paper documents practical constraints (cost, limited long-term memory handling) but does not empirically analyze sensitivity to unreliable structured-extraction pipelines or define the boundary conditions where hybrid memory fails; those specific failure modes are stated by the theory but are not evaluated in the paper and are therefore only peripherally addressed.

constraint_limitation.paper_title: Learning Knowledge Graph-based World Models of Textual Environments

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: The paper documents several limitations and failure modes: extractive approaches over-extract and fail when information is hidden; short-term-context tasks are less likely to benefit from complex world models; label imbalance and unreliable extraction harm valid-action prediction.
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: The theory includes explicit special-case constraints: little benefit when games are solvable from short-term context, and reduced/ disrupted benefits when memory construction (e.g., triplet extraction) is highly unreliable.
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: These constraint statements largely overlap with caveats and failure modes the paper already reports (hidden/latent information, short-horizon tasks, unreliable extraction). The law's constraints re-state and generalize those practical limitations rather than introduce wholly new constraints.

constraint_limitation.paper_title: Generative Agents: Interactive Simulacra of Human Behavior

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: The paper documents several limitations and failure modes: retrieval failures and incomplete retrieval, occasional hallucinated embellishments, mis-specified environmental norms causing erratic behavior, computational/cost scalability issues, and vulnerability to prompt- and memory-hacking; it also notes limited evaluation timescale and instruction-tuning biases.
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: The Hybrid Memory Synergy Law explicitly includes constraints: little benefit for tasks solvable with short-term context or trivial mappings, and degraded benefits if structured-memory construction (e.g., triplet extraction or KG building) is highly unreliable or noisy.
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: The paper already reports retrieval and hallucination-related limits and notes scalability and robustness concerns, but it does not specifically study the failure regime where an unreliable structured-memory construction pipeline (noisy triplet/KG extraction) negates the benefit of combining structured and episodic memories; this specific constraint is mentioned by the law but not empirically explored in the paper.

constraint_limitation.paper_title: Monte Carlo Planning with Large Language Model for Text-Based Game Agents

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: The paper explicitly notes limitations: truncated in-trial memory can miss distant clues (needle-in-a-haystack), input-window constraints require truncation/compression, unreliable or absent cross-trial memory reduces performance (shown by ablations), and LLM-alone agents can loop without effective exploration strategies.
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: It documents practical failure regimes and cautions that the chosen short in-trial memory window and LLM input constraints limit performance on puzzles requiring distant context; ablations quantify sensitivity to the memory modules and dynamic pruning.
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: These limitations overlap with parts of the theory's special cases (e.g., little benefit in short-horizon games, reduced gains if memory construction is poor), but the paper does not evaluate structured-memory-specific failure modes (e.g., noisy triplet extraction), so the theory's structured-memory constraint is not examined here.

constraint_limitation.paper_title: Evaluating and Enhancing LLMs Agent based on Theory of Mind in Guandan (搜蛋): A Multi-Player Cooperative Game under Imperfect Information

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: The paper empirically shows concrete limitations of vanilla LLM-agent approaches in Guandan: (1) severe difficulty processing very long, dynamic valid-action lists (agents preferentially select from first few items), (2) reliance on an external RL-based action recommender to reduce action-space burden, (3) persistent performance gap to a SOTA RL agent (Danzero+) for most models, and (4) sensitivity to player position and to top-k selection hyperparameters (smaller k reduces ToM benefit).
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: The hybrid-memory theory lists special cases where memory benefits are small (short-horizon/trivial mapping games) and warns that unreliable memory extraction reduces benefit. The paper introduces complementary, concrete operational constraints that degrade LLM-agent performance (long action lists, need for tool assistance, limited ToM gain when candidate set is too small).
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: The paper provides explicit empirical constraints relevant to whether memory-like augmentations will help: it documents that without tooling to prune action lists, LLMs fail to exploit planning/ToM effectively — an operational limitation orthogonal to but relevant for assessing hybrid-memory claims. These constraints are empirically explicit in the paper but not previously established for Guandan.

constraint_limitation.paper_title: Keep CALM and Explore: Language Models for Action Generation in Text-based Games

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: The paper documents several practical limitations and constraints: CALM confers little benefit if k is too small (misses complex actions), pretraining is essential (random-init model performs poorly), some games receive no benefit, and reducing the action set can in principle omit key actions (they analyze max-in-episode vs learned averages). It also notes dataset/noise issues (ClubFloyd transcripts contain mistakes) and tradeoffs between precision and recall as k varies.
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: The Hybrid Memory Synergy Law includes constraints: hybrid benefits are small for games solvable with short-term context and are degraded if structured-memory construction (e.g., triplet extraction) is highly unreliable.
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: The short-term-context caveat partially overlaps with the paper's empirical observation that some games show little benefit, which the paper treats as a peripheral finding; however, the specific constraint that unreliable structured-memory extraction will disrupt hybrid benefits is not investigated or evidenced in the paper and is a new, plausible limitation not analyzed here.

constraint_limitation.paper_title: Pre-trained Language Models as Prior Knowledge for Playing Text-based Games

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: The paper reports heterogeneous gains across games (some games get little or no improvement), TDQN benefits less than DRRN, fine-tuned LM transfer is imperfect, and stronger exploration strategies are still necessary to discover higher-score trajectories; it explicitly notes tasks solvable via short-term context or memorization may not benefit from richer priors.
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: The theory lists concrete constraints: hybrid memory offers little benefit on tasks solvable with short-term context or trivial observation-action mappings, and benefits degrade if structured-memory construction (e.g., noisy triplet extraction) is unreliable.
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: The 'little benefit on short/simple tasks' constraint is supported by the paper's heterogeneous results and is thus an explicit peripheral limitation already visible; however the specific failure mode tied to unreliable structured-memory construction (pipeline noise) is not discussed or evaluated in the paper and is an additional practical constraint introduced by the theory.

constraint_limitation.paper_title: Deciphering Digital Detectives: Understanding LLM Behaviors and Capabilities in Multi-Agent Mystery Games

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: The paper explicitly reports some limitations: memoryless agents gain less information about others, gains are small when memory is unnecessary, results vary due to LLM stochasticity, and experiments are confined to a Chinese dataset with reproducibility and cost caveats.
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: The theory states explicit constraints: little benefit in trivial short-horizon tasks and reduced or disrupted gains when structured-memory construction (e.g., triplet extraction) is highly unreliable.
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: While the paper documents that memory brings little benefit when short-term context suffices, it does not evaluate failure modes caused by unreliable structured-memory construction; the theory's explicit failure mode regarding noisy/failed structured-memory is not tested but is a plausible, derivable caution.

constraint_limitation.paper_title: Knowledge-enhanced Agents for Interactive Text Games

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: The paper reports explicit failure modes and limits: affordance injection can hurt performance when affordances are irrelevant or noisy (13/40 cases), naively concatenating affordances or using a separate encoder can introduce interference, MCA helps only modestly and can sometimes degrade performance, and benefits depend on affordance quality and task relevance.
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: It identifies concrete negative regimes and engineering constraints (e.g., prefer adding affordances into structured KGs for KG-A2C rather than raw input concatenation) and quantifies when injections backfire across models and tasks.
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: Those practical constraints and failure regimes are newly documented for this set of models and ScienceWorld tasks; they are important empirical findings but are broadly consistent with expected issues (irrelevant/noisy external knowledge causing interference).

constraint_limitation.paper_title: A Mixture-of-Experts Approach to Few-Shot Task Transfer in Open-Ended Text Worlds

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: The paper identifies concrete limitations of its MoE design: premature convergence to early-reward experts, expert starvation, degraded performance when relevant experts are removed (adversarial setup), and transient slowdown when many distractor experts are present; it proposes epsilon-greedy sampling and a flattening loss to mitigate these.
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: The theory lists additional constraints specific to hybrid structured+episodic memory (e.g., minimal benefit for tasks solvable with short-term context; benefits reduced if structured-memory extraction is highly unreliable), which are not empirically measured in the paper.
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: While the paper documents failure regimes for the MoE architecture, the particular constraints tied to unreliable triplet extraction or to interaction effects between structured and episodic memory are not analyzed here and thus represent new, untested limitations relative to the paper.

constraint_limitation.paper_title: Thespian: Multi-Character Text Role-Playing Game Agents

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: The paper documents specific limitations and failure modes of its architecture and ablations: attention over prompts or over softmaxed probabilities failed in ablations, softmaxing before fusion degraded performance, random prompts produce poor behavior, unfrozen retraining causes catastrophic forgetting, and pretraining biases (map layout) influence attention weights.
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: The theory lists constraints for the hybrid-memory claim: limited benefit in tasks solvable with short-term context and degraded gains when memory construction (e.g., triplet extraction) is unreliable.
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: Some practical constraints (catastrophic forgetting, attention-design sensitivity, environment bias) are explicitly observed in the paper, but the specific constraints about unreliable structured/episodic memory construction and task classes with negligible benefit are not studied here and thus are not established by this paper; they are plausible but not demonstrated.

constraint_limitation.paper_title: diff History for Neural Language Agents

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: The paper explicitly lists limitations: diff requires observations expressible in structured textual form, may hurt or increase token count in narrative/unstructured observations, and therefore may not help tasks lacking inherent observational structure; it also notes the assumption that observations/actions are available as text.
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: The theory adds constraints/special cases: negligible benefit for tasks solvable from short-term context and reduced/disrupted benefits when structured-memory construction (e.g., triplet extraction) is highly unreliable or noisy.
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: The paper already documents environment-structure-related failure modes and the need for textual observations; the specific caveat that noisy/ unreliable construction of explicit structured memory will diminish hybrid-memory benefits is not empirically analyzed in the paper but follows plausibly from its observations about reliance on structured signal.

constraint_limitation.paper_title: Language Models are Few-Shot Butlers

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: The paper documents practical limitations: iterated action modeling requires an initial non-zero success rate (hence a few demonstrations to bootstrap), contexts are limited by a sliding window (max sequence length), only successful trajectories are used for fine-tuning, and decoding/hyperparameter choices affect outcomes.
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: The theory lists special-case constraints such as negligible benefit on tasks solvable with short-term context and degraded or disrupted gains when structured-memory construction (e.g., triplet extraction) is highly unreliable or noisy.
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: While the paper reports operational constraints for IAM and context-size limits, it does not analyze failure modes specific to constructing or using explicit structured memory (e.g., extraction noise, incorrect triplets), so the structured-memory-specific limitations in the theory are not present in the paper.

constraint_limitation.paper_title: RAP: Retrieval-Augmented Planning with Contextual Memory for Multimodal LLM Agents

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: The paper acknowledges environment-dependent design choices (adaptive component weights), shows retrieval can add noise if full trajectories are used (hence windowing), and reports varying gains by task complexity; it provides ablations on retrieval modes and embeddings but lacks analysis of structured-memory construction failures.
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: The theory explicitly states constraints: hybrid memory yields little benefit in trivial short-horizon tasks, and benefits are reduced or disrupted if structured-memory construction (e.g., triplet extraction) is highly unreliable or noisy.
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: The specific limitation concerning the fragility of constructed structured/relational memories and their failure modes is not analyzed or evidenced in the paper and therefore is a new, untested constraint relative to RAP's results.

conceptual_reframing_abstraction.paper_title: AriGraph: Learning Knowledge Graph World Models with Episodic Memory for LLM Agents

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: The paper introduces a formal abstraction of memory as an integrated graph world model G=(V_s,E_s,V_e,E_e), episodic edges connecting semantic triplets to observations, and a two-stage retrieval + working-memory pipeline that separates planning and decision making.
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: The theory reframes agent memory design as a principled hybrid of structured (relational) and episodic (trajectory/observation) stores whose purposeful integration into working memory is the organizing principle for robust LLM agent cognition in text games.
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: That conceptual reframing and the specific graph abstraction are the central methodological contribution of the paper and are explicitly developed and used; the theory's abstraction aligns directly with and is established by the paper.

conceptual_reframing_abstraction.paper_title: A Survey on Large Language Model-Based Game Agents

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: The survey advances a clear conceptual architecture for LLMGAs (memory, reasoning, perception–action), and enumerates memory abstractions (working vs long-term, chunks/KV/trees/graphs/parametric) and associated operations (consolidation, structuring, retrieval).
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: The theory reframes agent design by centering a hybrid structured+episodic memory system with principled retrieval into working memory as the key organizing abstraction for robust, generalizable LLM agent behavior in text games.
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: The paper already provides the canonical architecture and catalog of memory abstractions; centering the hybrid structured+episodic integration as the singular organizing lens is therefore an incremental reframing rather than a wholly new conceptual abstraction relative to the survey's explicit content.

conceptual_reframing_abstraction.paper_title: Knowledge-enhanced Agents for Interactive Text Games

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: The paper frames external knowledge for interactive agents along two axes—structured affordance knowledge (KG-style) and episodic action-memory (MCA)—and along injection modalities (KG triples, concatenation, separate encoders, pretraining), presenting an organizing taxonomy for knowledge injection experiments.
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: This concise taxonomy (knowledge type × injection strategy × agent class) is made explicit and used to guide experimental comparisons and practical recommendations.
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: The framing is a useful, practical abstraction that organizes prior disparate techniques into a comparative experimental lens, but it is an incremental conceptual organization rather than a fundamentally new theoretical abstraction.

conceptual_reframing_abstraction.paper_title: Evaluating and Enhancing LLMs Agent based on Theory of Mind in Guandan (搜蛋): A Multi-Player Cooperative Game under Imperfect Information

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: The paper proposes a modular conceptualization for LLM-based embodied agents in imperfect-information games (State Interpreter, ToM Planner, Plan Evaluator) and frames planning in orders of ToM (zero/first/second order) as distinct algorithmic levels. It treats external tool invocation (action recommender) as a first-class module integrated into planning.
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: The hybrid-memory theory reframes agent memory as an explicit hybrid of structured (knowledge-graph/semantic) and episodic (trajectory/reflective) stores whose principled retrieval synergizes to solve partial observability and long-horizon credit assignment. The paper does not adopt that specific memory abstraction, but does introduce an abstraction of ToM-order planning and tool-augmented modular agents.
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: The paper's conceptual contribution is the ToM-order abstraction and modular pipeline for integrating belief inference and external tools into LLM decision loops; this reframing is explicitly articulated and evaluated in the paper but is distinct from the hybrid structured/episodic-memory abstraction in the theory statement.

conceptual_reframing_abstraction.paper_title: Graph Constrained Reinforcement Learning for Natural Language Action Spaces

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: The paper advances a conceptual reframing for IF agents: represent partially observed textual worlds as structured knowledge graphs (triples), use GATs to embed that structure, and treat language actions as template-structured generation constrained by a graph mask that binds state to generation.
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: The theory generalizes this into an abstract hybrid-memory architecture that organizes agent memory into complementary structured (relational) and episodic (trajectory/reflective) stores with principled retrieval into working memory for decision-making, especially for LLM-based agents.
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: The hybrid-memory abstraction is a natural conceptual generalization of the paper's structured-state idea but is not explicitly proposed or instantiated in the paper; therefore the reframing is a derivable but unstated conceptual extension of the paper's contributions.

conceptual_reframing_abstraction.paper_title: MemInsight: Autonomous Memory Augmentation for LLM Agents

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: The paper presents an organizing abstraction for agent memory: memory entries augmented with attribute–value pairs, organized by perspective (entity vs. conversation), granularity (turn vs. session), and prioritized annotations feeding refined vs. comprehensive retrieval strategies.
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: The theory reframes agent memory design as a broader dichotomy between structured (semantic/relational) and episodic (trajectory/experiential) memories and prescribes their synergistic, principled retrieval into working memory as a general architectural principle for robust agents in partially observable, long-horizon text environments.
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: MemInsight already offers a new attribute-centric abstraction for structured memory, but it does not formalize or center the structured-vs-episodic dichotomy as a general architectural law for interactive text-game agents; that higher-level reframing is an extension beyond the paper's framing.

conceptual_reframing_abstraction.paper_title: Generative Agents: Interactive Simulacra of Human Behavior

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: The paper introduces and formalizes new abstractions for agent memory and cognition: a natural-language memory stream of timestamped observations, periodic reflections (recursive synthesis of memories into higher-level insights), plans-as-memories with hierarchical decomposition, and an environment tree for grounding; it frames agent coherence as arising from storage, retrieval, reflection, and planning.
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: The Hybrid Memory Synergy Law reframes agent memory architecture as an explicit dual-modality abstraction (structured semantic/graph state + episodic trajectory/reflective memory) and centers principled joint retrieval into working memory as the organizing principle for robust, generalizable LLM-agent performance in partial-observability, long-horizon tasks.
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: The paper contributes a substantial conceptual reframing (memory stream + reflections + planning) but does not present the dual-modality (semantic KG + episodic traces) abstraction or treat a semantic knowledge-graph as a central retrievable memory modality integrated with episodic traces; that particular abstraction and emphasis on KG+episodic integration is not developed in the paper.

conceptual_reframing_abstraction.paper_title: Monte Carlo Planning with Large Language Model for Text-Based Game Agents

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: The paper introduces and uses a concise conceptual abstraction partitioning memory for LLM agents into in-trial (short trajectory window / working memory) and cross-trial (episodic reflection) components and frames MC-DML as simulating human-like retention and reflection within MCTS planning.
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: It formalizes how those two memory abstractions are encoded, retrieved, and injected into LLM prompts to form a dynamic prior inside PUCT selection.
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: The broader conceptual reframing proposed by the theory — treating structured relational state representations and episodic traces as a single hybrid memory abstraction with principled retrieval algorithms — is not adopted or operationalized in the paper and thus remains a conceptual extension beyond the paper's contribution.

conceptual_reframing_abstraction.paper_title: RAP: Retrieval-Augmented Planning with Contextual Memory for Multimodal LLM Agents

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: The paper provides an operational abstraction of memory as structured logs (task, overall plan, trajectory) and describes a modular pipeline (Memory, Reasoner, Retriever, Executor) that reframes episodic retrieval as supplying in‑context learning exemplars to the agent.
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: The theory reframes agent memory conceptually as a two-component hybrid abstraction (structured relational memory + episodic instance memory) with principled joint retrieval and integration as the organizing lens for agent cognition in sequential, partially observable tasks.
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: The explicit conceptual reframing to a canonical hybrid KG+episodic abstraction and the prescription to treat joint retrieval as the primary organizing abstraction is not formalized or advocated in the paper, although some of the paper's design choices (task/plan fields) hint toward mixing structured cues with episodic instances.

conceptual_reframing_abstraction.paper_title: Learning Dynamic Belief Graphs to Generalize on Text-Based Games

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: The paper reframes learned latent belief graphs as an inductive-bias memory mechanism for partially observable text games: it treats a dynamic adjacency tensor as the agent’s structured memory and formalizes graph construction and update as a learned memory operation used during planning and action selection.
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: The theory elevates this into a broader abstraction: a hybrid-memory design pattern that prescribes combining explicit relational (KG-like) representations with episodic (trajectory/experience) memory and principled retrieval into working memory for LLM agents.
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: The paper embodies and defends the conceptual reframing of graphs-as-memory, but it does not formalize or evaluate the proposed hybrid-memory abstraction combining separate episodic stores with structured graphs; that explicit abstraction is an extension derivable from but not present in the paper.

conceptual_reframing_abstraction.paper_title: Large Language Models Are Neurosymbolic Reasoners

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: The paper reframes LLM agents that call external symbolic modules as 'neurosymbolic reasoners' and provides an operational abstraction (role initialization, action-query prompting, constrained prompts) for integrating tools with LLMs in interactive text-game agents.
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: The Hybrid Memory Synergy Law proposes a different conceptual abstraction: treating agent cognition as an interacting hybrid memory system (structured relational memory + episodic memory) whose principled retrieval into working memory is the core organizing principle for robust, generalizable agent behavior.
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: The paper does not introduce or adopt this hybrid memory abstraction — it focuses on prompting and tool integration rather than designing or framing agents around explicit structured and episodic memory subsystems — so this reframing is novel with respect to the paper.

conceptual_reframing_abstraction.paper_title: Deciphering Digital Detectives: Understanding LLM Behaviors and Capabilities in Multi-Agent Mystery Games

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: The paper frames improvements in terms of concrete modules (Memory Retriever, Self-Refinement, Self-Verification) and practical engineering choices, but it does not articulate a high-level taxonomy that separates and prescribes structured versus episodic memory as the central design principle.
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: The theory reframes agent memory design as an abstraction that separates structured relational memory and episodic trajectory/reflective memory and prescribes their purposeful integration and retrieval as the organizing principle for robust, generalizable LLM agents in text games.
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: This conceptual reframing (explicit structured-vs-episodic decomposition and its primacy as a design law) is not present in the paper, which remains at the level of unstructured episodic retrieval plus iterative refinement; thus the abstraction is new relative to this work.

conceptual_reframing_abstraction.paper_title: Positive Experience Reflection for Agents in Interactive Text Environments

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: The paper reframes reflection as a managed-memory design (short-term and long-term reflection buffers) and introduces the conceptual distinction of reflecting on positive versus negative experiences as an architectural choice for LLM agents.
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: The theory reframes agent memory architecture at a higher abstraction: a hybrid two-component memory (structured relational state + episodic trajectory/reflection store) whose principled integration and retrieval policy is central to robust, generalizable agent behaviour.
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: The paper advances the managed-episodic reflection abstraction but does not propose or analyze the higher-level hybrid structured+episodic memory abstraction or its retrieval policies; that reframing is not present in the paper.

conceptual_reframing_abstraction.paper_title: Behavior Cloned Transformers are Neurosymbolic Reasoners

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: The paper frames the contribution operationally as neurosymbolic action-space injection—treating symbolic modules as callable actions made available to sequence models—rather than as an explicit memory-systems abstraction.
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: The theory reframes LLM-agent augmentation as the principled integration of two complementary memory abstractions—structured relational memory and episodic trajectory/reflective memory—centered on retrieval policies and working-memory composition as the core design principle for robust generalization.
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: This conceptual reframing (treating symbolic/tool augmentation and trajectory data as distinct memory systems whose joint retrieval is the key architectural primitive) is not presented in the paper and thus constitutes a novel abstraction relative to the work.

conceptual_reframing_abstraction.paper_title: A Mixture-of-Experts Approach to Few-Shot Task Transfer in Open-Ended Text Worlds

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: The paper operationalizes persistent structured state as a knowledge graph (KG) and treats pre-trained, story-shaped KG-A2C agents as modular, reusable experts assembled via an attention-based MoE for transfer; it therefore adopts a modular policy/memory engineering view within KG-A2C frameworks.
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: The theory reframes agent design at a higher abstraction: explicitly positing a canonical hybrid-memory architecture (structured relational memory + episodic trajectory/reflection memory) whose principled retrieval into working memory is the organizing design principle for robust, generalizable LLM agents in text games.
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: This explicit hybrid-memory conceptual reframing is not present in the paper (which lacks an episodic-memory substrate or the associated abstract framing), so the reframing is a novel conceptual contribution beyond what the paper presents.

conceptual_reframing_abstraction.paper_title: Reasoning, Memorization, and Fine-Tuning Language Models for Non-Cooperative Games

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: The paper reframes LLM game solving as a multi-agent system of specialized language-model agents that exchange compact, consistent text-based representations (summaries/areas) via a tree-of-thought interaction pattern; this is presented as an organizing abstraction for reducing memory growth and improving reasoning.
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: The theory proposes a different abstraction: modeling agent memory as two orthogonal, complementary systems (structured relational state stores and episodic trajectory/experience stores) with principled retrieval into a working memory as the central organizing design for robust LLM agents.
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: The two-tier memory abstraction (structured + episodic) and its centrality to agent design are not introduced or operationalized in the paper; this conceptual reframing is therefore novel relative to the paper's contributions.

conceptual_reframing_abstraction.paper_title: Reflexion: Language Agents with Verbal Reinforcement Learning

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: Reflexion reframes policy improvement for language agents as 'verbal reinforcement learning' and gives an abstraction separating Actor, Evaluator, and Self-Reflection plus a short-term (trajectory) vs long-term (textual reflections) memory decomposition.
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: The theory offers a different conceptual abstraction: treating the agent memory substrate as a hybrid composed of explicit structured semantic/graph state representations jointly with episodic reflective traces and emphasizing principled retrieval/integration into working memory as the organizing principle for robust LLM agents.
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: While Reflexion introduces a verbal-reinforcement abstraction and episodic vs short-term decomposition, it does not propose the specific hybrid structured+episodic memory abstraction or its retrieval-centered organization; that reframing is not advanced in the paper and is therefore novel relative to Reflexion.

conceptual_reframing_abstraction.paper_title: Learning Knowledge Graph-based World Models of Textual Environments

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: The paper introduces novel abstractions: casting KG generation as predicting graph differences and framing both graph diffs and valid actions as a Set-of-Sequences generation problem; it also conceptually treats mapping and acting as a joint, correlated problem solved by multi-task learning.
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: The theory reframes agent memory architecture at a higher level: it posits a principled hybrid-memory abstraction where structured relational state and episodic trajectory/reflective memory are distinct but integrable subsystems whose coordinated retrieval into working memory is the primary organizing principle for robust LLM agent behavior.
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: That particular conceptual reframing (structured vs episodic memory as the canonical complementary abstraction for LLM agents, with principled retrieval integration) is not present in the paper and represents a new organizing abstraction beyond the paper's Set-of-Sequences and graph-diff reframings.

conceptual_reframing_abstraction.paper_title: Language Models are Few-Shot Butlers

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: The paper's organizing abstraction is 'action modeling' for language models plus the iterated action modeling loop (imitation → interaction → fine-tune); it does not adopt an explicit memory-systems taxonomy separating structured vs episodic memory as its core lens.
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: The theory reframes agent design through a memory-systems abstraction that explicitly distinguishes structured relational state representations from episodic trajectory/reflective memory and emphasizes principled retrieval into working memory as the organizing design principle.
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: That conceptual reframing (treating LLM agents in text games as hybrid structured+episodic memory systems with retrieval policies as the central abstraction) is not present in the paper and represents a different organizing perspective.

conceptual_reframing_abstraction.paper_title: Thespian: Multi-Character Text Role-Playing Game Agents

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: The paper offers a distinct conceptual reframing for text-RL by distinguishing 'character' agents from 'actor/thespian' agents, formalizing soft character prompts and per-character policy heads, and framing few-shot character composition as attention-based policy fusion.
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: The theory proposes a different abstraction: framing agent cognition and generalization in terms of a hybrid memory architecture composed of structured relational state and episodic trajectory/reflective stores with principled retrieval into working memory as the organizing lens.
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: The hybrid-memory abstraction and its use as the primary organizing concept for robust LLM-agent behavior is not present in the paper, so this conceptual reframing is novel relative to the paper's contributions.

conceptual_reframing_abstraction.paper_title: diff History for Neural Language Agents

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: The paper introduces a concrete conceptual reframing: view interaction history not as raw text but as a task-agnostic, human-interpretable abstraction of relational text deltas (diffs) that alter how prompts are constructed and used for LM agents.
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: The theory proposes a higher-level conceptual abstraction decomposing agent memory into two complementary components—structured relational memory and episodic trajectory memory—and emphasizes principled retrieval and integration into working memory as an organizing design principle for robust LLM agents.
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: The paper promotes a practical abstraction (diffs) but does not adopt or argue for the broader conceptual decomposition of memory types with retrieval-integration policies; that higher-level reframing (structured vs episodic memory as the organizing abstraction) is not present in the paper and thus is novel relative to it.

conceptual_reframing_abstraction.paper_title: Keep CALM and Explore: Language Models for Action Generation in Text-based Games

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: Conceptually, the paper reframes action generation in text games as a two-stage process: (1) produce a compact, context-conditioned candidate set via a language model (capturing linguistic priors) and (2) re-rank those candidates with RL to maximize reward. That reframing departs from purely rule-based or admissible-action handicaps.
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: The Hybrid Memory Synergy Law offers a different conceptual abstraction: treating agent memory as a hybrid system composed of structured relational state memory and episodic trajectory/reflective memory, with a principled retrieval and integration mechanism feeding working memory to LLM-based decision modules.
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: Recasting agent memory as an explicitly hybrid structured+episodic memory system and proposing retrieval/integration as a central organizing abstraction for LLM agents is not present in the paper; the paper uses unstructured LM context windows and compares to graph-based baselines but does not adopt or formalize this hybrid-memory abstraction.

conceptual_reframing_abstraction.paper_title: Pre-trained Language Models as Prior Knowledge for Playing Text-based Games
