dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: The paper reframes the design choice for text-game agents by treating pre-trained LMs as a source of linguistic, world and game-sense priors that can replace or complement engineered knowledge graphs and that should be integrated into RL agents as a representation module.
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: The theory proposes a different organizing abstraction: architect agent memory as an explicitly hybrid system (structured relational memory + episodic trajectory/reflective memory) with principled retrieval into working memory as the central design principle for robust, generalizable LLM-agent behavior.
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: The hybrid structured-vs-episodic memory abstraction and its promotion as the primary organizing lens is not developed or instantiated in the paper; the paper's LM-as-prior framing is related but does not instantiate this specific abstraction, so the reframing is novel relative to the paper.

empirical_synthesis_meta_regulariry.paper_title: AriGraph: Learning Knowledge Graph World Models with Episodic Memory for LLM Agents

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: The paper aggregates experiments across multiple games, NetHack, ablations, QA benchmarks, and token-efficiency analyses to show a consistent pattern: the hybrid AriGraph outperforms alternative memory designs and scales to harder tasks while maintaining retrieval/compute trade-offs.
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: The theory asserts this meta-regularity as a general law: hybrid structured+episodic memory reliably improves normalized performance, reduces errors, and increases sample efficiency/generalization across relevant benchmarks.
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: The paper already provides this empirical synthesis and demonstrates the consistent superiority of the hybrid approach across evaluated settings; therefore the meta-regularity is explicitly established rather than novel relative to this work.

empirical_synthesis_meta_regulariry.paper_title: Learning Dynamic Belief Graphs to Generalize on Text-Based Games

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: Within this paper there is a clear empirical regularity: graph-structured belief representations (learned via OG/COC pretraining and used by GATA) consistently improve policy learning and test-time generalization across the TextWorld suites tested, and ground-truth graphs yield an even stronger systematic advantage.
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: The theory asserts a higher-level meta-regularity that deliberately combining structured and episodic memories yields robust, sample-efficient, and generalizable LLM agents across text-game settings; the paper provides empirical support for the structured-memory half but does not synthesize evidence for the hybrid (structured+episodic) meta-regularity.
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: The full hybrid-memory meta-regularity (structured+episodic synergy) is not evidenced or synthesized in the paper and thus represents a novel, peripheral extension relative to the paper’s demonstrated empirical pattern.

empirical_synthesis_meta_regulariry.paper_title: MemInsight: Autonomous Memory Augmentation for LLM Agents

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: The paper provides empirical evidence across three dialogue tasks and two datasets that attribute-based augmentations and priority strategies produce consistent improvements in retrieval metrics and several downstream evaluations, indicating a repeatable pattern within the evaluated dialogue domain.
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: The theory asserts a broader meta-regularity: hybrid structured+episodic memory reliably produces robustness, sample-efficiency, and generalization advantages across text-based game environments and tasks involving partial observability and long-horizon planning.
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: While the paper documents a consistent effect of structured augmentations across dialogue tasks, it does not synthesize evidence across the distinct memory paradigms (structured vs episodic) nor across interactive game domains; the claimed cross-domain meta-regularity is therefore not empirically established by the paper.

empirical_synthesis_meta_regulariry.paper_title: Behavior Cloned Transformers are Neurosymbolic Reasoners

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: The paper reports a clear empirical regularity: across 100 test games per benchmark, behavior-cloned transformers trained on gold trajectories gain large, consistent performance and efficiency improvements when symbolic-module actions are injected, while the DRRN baseline does not exhibit comparable gains under the same conditions.
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: The theory asserts a broader meta-regularity that joint availability and principled retrieval of structured and episodic memory yields robust, sample-efficient, and generalizable agent behavior across text-game tasks, outperforming single-memory or unstructured-history approaches.
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: The paper provides strong empirical evidence for one axis (structured symbolic tools + trajectory supervision) but does not empirically evaluate episodic-memory retrieval at inference or contrast against unstructured full-history retrieval; therefore the fuller meta-regularity about hybrid structured+episodic memory is not demonstrated and must be inferred or tested in future work.

empirical_synthesis_meta_regulariry.paper_title: Reflexion: Language Agents with Verbal Reinforcement Learning

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: The paper synthesizes empirical evidence across several task families to conclude a meta-regularity: verbal self-reflection (episodic textual memory) consistently improves sample efficiency and task performance across decision-making, reasoning, and code generation, supported by ablations and model comparisons.
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: The theory posits a broader empirical regularity that hybrids of structured and episodic memory produce systematically better robustness, generalization, and sample efficiency than either memory alone or naive full-history retrieval across text-game-style partial-observability tasks.
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: Reflexion provides cross-task synthesis for episodic/verbal memory benefits but contains no empirical synthesis showing the additional advantage of combining structured and episodic memories; the hybrid meta-regularity is therefore not demonstrated in the paper and remains a derivable hypothesis rather than an established empirical regularity.

empirical_synthesis_meta_regulariry.paper_title: Learning Knowledge Graph-based World Models of Textual Environments

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: Empirically, the paper synthesizes across multiple test games to show a regular pattern: structured knowledge-graph based world models (Worldformer) using graph-diff prediction, multi-task learning, and SOS loss outperform several strong baselines on KG and valid-action prediction metrics across 9 unseen games; ablations quantify the importance of each component.
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: The theory asserts a broader meta-regularity: across agents and environments, combining structured and episodic memories yields consistent improvements in robustness, sample efficiency, error rates, and generalization relative to single-memory or unstructured-history agents.
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: The paper's empirical synthesis supports the value of structured memory but does not provide cross-study or cross-architecture evidence about hybrid structured+episodic memory; the proposed universal meta-regularity for hybrid memories is therefore not established here and remains an extension beyond the paper's synthesized results.

empirical_synthesis_meta_regulariry.paper_title: Generative Agents: Interactive Simulacra of Human Behavior

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: The paper synthesizes evidence internally across controlled ablations and an end-to-end multi-agent deployment to show a consistent pattern: inclusion of observation recording, reflection synthesis, and planning each add measurable believability and enable emergent social phenomena; statistical analysis (TrueSkill, Kruskal-Wallis, Dunn tests) supports these internal comparisons.
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: The Hybrid Memory Synergy Law asserts a broader meta-regularity across tasks and domains: that combining structured and episodic memories systematically improves normalized/game performance, error rates, sample efficiency, and generalization relative to single-modality or unstructured-memory baselines.
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: The paper provides an empirical synthesis across internal architectural ablations and emergent outcomes but does not establish the cross-domain meta-regularity for structured+episodic hybrids (nor synthesize evidence across multiple external studies or game domains); the law's cross-task meta-regularity is therefore not empirically supported here.

empirical_synthesis_meta_regulariry.paper_title: Monte Carlo Planning with Large Language Model for Text-Based Game Agents

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: Empirically, the paper synthesizes results over nine games showing a consistent pattern: adding cross-trial reflections and short in-trial context to an LLM prior inside MCTS improves scores, addresses bottlenecks, and raises initial planning performance relative to several baselines; ablations confirm the importance of both memory components.
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: It supplies an empirical meta-regularity confined to the Jericho suite that episodic reflections plus limited trajectory context systematically improve LLM-guided MCTS performance across diverse text-game challenges.
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: The theory posits a stronger meta-regularity that this hybrid (structured+episodic) approach will systematically outperform single-memory or unstructured-history agents across broader text-game domains; the paper supports only the episodic+in-trial part of that claim, so the full hybrid meta-regularity is not empirically established here.

empirical_synthesis_meta_regulariry.paper_title: Thespian: Multi-Character Text Role-Playing Game Agents

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: Within its experiments the paper synthesizes an empirical regularity: freezing a pre-trained core and training a small attention-based fusion over per-character logits yields faster few-shot learning of a blended character and preserves prior behaviors, whereas unfrozen retraining is slower and causes forgetting.
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: The theory asserts a broader meta-regularity that hybrid structured+episodic memory consistently outperforms either memory alone and beats unstructured full-history retrieval across methods and environments.
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: The paper provides a single-system internal synthesis about policy fusion but does not aggregate cross-study evidence to support a general meta-regularity about hybrid memories; extrapolating the broader regularity from this paper would be premature, so the theory-level synthesis is not supported here.

empirical_synthesis_meta_regulariry.paper_title: Evaluating and Enhancing LLMs Agent based on Theory of Mind in Guandan (搜蛋): A Multi-Player Cooperative Game under Imperfect Information

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: The paper runs a multi-model empirical study (several open-source and closed-source LLMs) and synthesizes results showing (a) ToM planning consistently improves average scores across most models and opponents, (b) action-recommender tooling markedly improves LLM performance in high-branching action settings, and (c) GPT-4 with ToM+tooling approaches but does not always surpass a SOTA RL agent.
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: The hybrid-memory statement predicts a broad empirical regularity that combining structured and episodic memories improves robustness, sample efficiency and generalization. The paper introduces a different empirical regularity: that belief/ToM prompting plus an external action-pruning tool produces consistent gains across diverse LLMs in a complex text-game domain.
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: The paper provides a new cross-model empirical synthesis establishing that ToM-style belief inference and a lightweight external recommender together yield consistent improvements in a multi-agent imperfect-information card game; this is an empirical regularity relevant to augmentation strategies but does not test or confirm the hybrid structured+episodic memory synergy the theory asserts.

empirical_synthesis_meta_regulariry.paper_title: Keep CALM and Explore: Language Models for Action Generation in Text-based Games

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: Empirically the paper synthesizes evidence across 28 Jericho games showing that pretrained LM action priors trained on diverse human transcripts generalize to unseen games and, when combined with RL, improve average normalized performance versus several baselines; it quantifies precision/recall behavior across k and reports per-game variability.
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: The Hybrid Memory Synergy Law claims a meta-regularity: across text-game environments with partial observability and combinatorial action spaces, principled integration of structured and episodic memories produces robust, sample-efficient, and generalizable improvements over single-memory or naive full-history approaches.
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: While the paper provides an empirical regularity about LM priors aiding RL across many games, it does not synthesize evidence about the joint contribution of structured and episodic memories; the specific meta-regularity about their combined, systemic benefit is not established here and would require additional cross-method experiments.

empirical_synthesis_meta_regulariry.paper_title: RAP: Retrieval-Augmented Planning with Contextual Memory for Multimodal LLM Agents

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: The paper synthesizes empirical evidence across four benchmarks and multiple models, reporting consistent gains from episodic retrieval with adaptive retrieval keys, windowing, and modality-specific embeddings, and presents ablations that identify components contributing to gains.
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: The theory asserts a cross-domain meta-regularity: purposeful hybridization of structured and episodic memory will consistently yield robust gains in normalized performance, error reduction, sample efficiency, and generalization relative to single-type or unstructured-history baselines.
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: While the paper establishes a cross-benchmark regularity for episodic retrieval, it does not supply empirical synthesis demonstrating the incremental, consistent benefit of adding an explicit structured/relational memory to episodic logs; the hybrid meta-regularity is therefore not supported by the paper's experiments.

empirical_synthesis_meta_regulariry.paper_title: Large Language Models Are Neurosymbolic Reasoners

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: The paper provides an empirical dataset showing that LLM agents augmented with symbolic modules and constrained prompts achieve strong, consistent performance across four symbolic tasks, with quantitative comparisons to DRRN and behavior-cloned baselines within the same benchmark suite.
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: The theory asserts a meta-regularity: across agents, tasks, and environments, augmenting LLM agents with a hybrid of structured and episodic memory will consistently yield improved robustness, sample efficiency, and generalization relative to single-memory or unstructured-history approaches.
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: While the paper synthesizes evidence that tool-augmented LLMs perform well on several symbolic tasks, it does not synthesize cross-study evidence about hybrid memory systems nor present experiments comparing hybrid-memory agents to alternatives; thus the proposed cross-cutting meta-regularity for hybrid memories is not supported by this paper and is a novel synthesis claim.

empirical_synthesis_meta_regulariry.paper_title: Graph Constrained Reinforcement Learning for Natural Language Action Spaces

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: The paper establishes an empirical regularity that structured knowledge-graph state representations combined with template action spaces and graph masking tend to improve learning speed and final scores across many IF games, and ablations quantify component contributions.
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: The theory posits a broader empirical synthesis: that systematically combining structured and episodic memory stores yields consistent gains in robustness, sample efficiency, reduced error rates, and out-of-distribution generalization over single-memory or unstructured-history baselines.
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: The paper supports the value of structured memory across many tasks but provides no empirical data on episodic memory or on hybrid-structured+episodic systems; thus the meta-regularity claimed for hybrid-memory systems is not demonstrated here and is a novel synthesis relative to this paper.

empirical_synthesis_meta_regulariry.paper_title: Deciphering Digital Detectives: Understanding LLM Behaviors and Capabilities in Multi-Agent Mystery Games

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: The paper supplies single-domain empirical evidence that MR+SR+SV improves information acquisition and reasoning in Jubensha, and it references related work, but it does not aggregate multi-study results to claim a cross-domain meta-regularity.
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: The theory asserts a meta-regularity: across text-game environments, hybrid structured+episodic memory systematically yields superior robustness, sample efficiency, and generalization compared to single or unstructured memories.
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: There is no multi-study synthesis or cross-environment aggregation in the paper to support this meta-regularity; claiming such a broad regularity goes beyond the paper's empirical scope and is therefore a genuinely new claim.

empirical_synthesis_meta_regulariry.paper_title: Knowledge-enhanced Agents for Interactive Text Games

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: The paper aggregates results across 40 model/task configurations and reports consistent empirical patterns: affordances improve action selection in a majority of cases (25/40), LMs tend to benefit more on average than RL agents, and injecting affordances into KGs is often superior to raw concatenation or separate encoders for KG-A2C.
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: It synthesizes these cross-model, cross-task findings into concrete recommendations (prefer affordances over MCA when limited, integrate affordances as KG triples, expect task-dependent variability), producing a meta-regularity about when and how these knowledge types help interactive agents.
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: This aggregated empirical regularity across agent classes and tasks—showing affordance-dominant benefits and injection-modality effects on ScienceWorld benchmarks—is a new synthesis specific to this experimental suite and was not previously established in the literature in this consolidated form.

empirical_synthesis_meta_regulariry.paper_title: Positive Experience Reflection for Agents in Interactive Text Environments

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: The paper aggregates results across 30 ScienceWorld tasks and three LLM families and reports a consistent empirical pattern: Sweet&Sour improves average success scores, shows larger relative gains on medium-difficulty tasks, and narrows model-size gaps compared to failure-only reflection.
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: The theory asserts a broader meta-regularity: hybrid structured+episodic memory yields robust, sample-efficient, and generalizable gains across text-game domains relative to single memory types or unstructured full-history retrieval.
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: The paper provides a clear empirical synthesis for episodic/positive-reflection benefits within ScienceWorld, but it does not provide empirical support for the broader hybrid structured+episodic regularity; the hybrid empirical claim is not established here.

empirical_synthesis_meta_regulariry.paper_title: A Mixture-of-Experts Approach to Few-Shot Task Transfer in Open-Ended Text Worlds

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: Within its experiments, the paper establishes an empirical regularity: an attention-weighted MoE of frozen KG-A2C experts plus a hot expert consistently speeds few-shot adaptation and improves zero-shot performance in its role-based LIGHT tasks; prior cited work shows KG memory and story-shaping can produce role-aligned behaviors but does not evaluate hybrid structured+episodic combinations.
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: The theory claims a broader meta-regularity across agent families and studies: systematic gains from jointly using structured and episodic memories versus single-memory or naive full-history retrieval approaches in text-game agents.
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: No cross-study synthesis or experiments involving episodic memory are presented in the paper, so the claimed meta-regularity (structured+episodic hybrid superiority as a general empirical law) is not supported here and is a genuinely new, untested claim relative to this paper.

empirical_synthesis_meta_regulariry.paper_title: Reasoning, Memorization, and Fine-Tuning Language Models for Non-Cooperative Games

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: Empirically, the paper establishes that structured summarization plus constrained area selection in a multi-agent tree-of-thought pipeline improves win rates and reduces errors on the tested game, and that task-specific fine-tuning with ≈1k examples yields further gains; it also reports the cost/performance trade-off.
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: The theory asserts a broader meta-regularity: across environments and tasks, agents that combine structured relational memory with episodic trajectory memory and retrieve both deliberately will systematically outperform agents with only one memory type or naive full-history retrieval.
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: The paper provides no multi-environment synthesis or controlled comparisons among structured-only, episodic-only, unstructured-only, and hybrid-memory agents, so the claimed meta-regularity is not supported by this paper and represents a genuinely new empirical hypothesis relative to the presented results.

empirical_synthesis_meta_regulariry.paper_title: diff History for Neural Language Agents

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: Within its experiments, the paper synthesizes evidence showing a consistent empirical pattern: diff-encoded histories provide denser learning signals, improve sample efficiency and generalization, and scale with data in NetHack, with large ablation effects versus full-text history.
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: The theory makes a broader meta-regularity claim that hybrid structured+episodic memory systems will systematically outperform single-memory or unstructured-history agents across text-game domains by overcoming partial observability and long-horizon credit assignment bottlenecks.
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: The paper provides a strong single-method empirical synthesis for one type of structured episodic encoding (diffs) but does not synthesize cross-method or cross-paper evidence comparing hybrid memory systems to alternatives; the general meta-regularity that hybrid memories will robustly outperform other designs across domains remains an untested, novel empirical claim.

empirical_synthesis_meta_regulariry.paper_title: Pre-trained Language Models as Prior Knowledge for Playing Text-based Games

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: The paper provides an empirical synthesis across 14 Jericho games showing a single fine-tuned LM component consistently improves or matches prior baselines on many games, yields better average normalized score, and reduces required environment interactions compared to several baselines.
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: The theory asserts a broader meta-regularity: across text-game environments, agents that integrate both structured and episodic memory into working memory will systematically outperform agents that use only one memory type or only unstructured full-history retrieval.
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: The paper's synthesis supports the regularity that LM priors help across many games, but it does not synthesize evidence for hybrid structured+episodic memory outperforming other architectures; the hybrid-memory meta-regularity is not demonstrated in the paper and therefore is a novel empirical claim.

These constraints are treated as recognized practical limitations with quantified effects across task types, model capacities, and extraction-quality regimes.
constraint_limitation.what_introduced: The theory enumerates two explicit special-case constraints as boundary conditions for the Hybrid Memory Synergy Law: (1) games solvable via short-term context or trivial observation-to-action mapping may show little benefit from hybrid memory, and (2) if memory construction (e.g., triplet extraction) is highly unreliable, benefits are reduced or disrupted.

theory_law_dict.theory_name: Hybrid Structured and Episodic Memory Enables Robust, Generalizable LLM Agent Performance in Text Games
theory_law_dict.theory_description: Language model agents for text-based games achieve superior robustness, sample efficiency, and generalization when augmented with hybrid memory systems that combine explicit structured (e.g., knowledge-graph or belief-graph) state representations and episodic (trajectory or reflection-based) memory. Structured memory provides compact, relational representations that support multi-hop reasoning, navigation, and action-space pruning, while episodic memory enables recall of detailed instructions, procedures, and successful/failed experiences. The synergistic integration and principled retrieval of both kinds of memories enables agents to overcome both partial observability and long-horizon credit assignment bottlenecks, outperforming agents with either memory alone, with unstructured memory (e.g., full text history), or with naive retrieval/summarization.
theory_law_dict.theory_statement: When both structured (e.g., knowledge-graph/semantic) and episodic (e.g., observation/trajectory/reflective) memory are available and purposefully integrated and retrieved into agent working memory, LLM-based agents achieve higher normalized/game performance, reduced error rates, and greater sample efficiency and generalization compared to agents using only one memory type or only unstructured full-history/retrieval.
theory_law_dict.domain_scope: Applies to LLM agents in text-based games with partial observability, combinatorial action spaces, and complex state transitions, especially in environments requiring multi-step planning, navigation, and long-term instruction following (e.g., TextWorld, NetHack, general text-based tasks)
special_cases: Games solvable via short-term context or trivial observation-to-action mapping may show little benefit.
If memory construction (e.g., triplet extraction) is highly unreliable, benefits are reduced/disrupted.

aggregated_results.paper_title: AriGraph: Learning Knowledge Graph World Models with Episodic Memory for LLM Agents

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: The paper reports limitations and task-specific effects: hybrid memory yields little advantage for trivial short-horizon tasks; episodic vs semantic contributions differ by task (episodic important for Cooking), and noisy/unreliable triplet extraction or low-quality LLM backbones degrade graph growth/update quality and performance.
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: The theory lists special cases: minimal benefit when short-term context suffices and disrupted gains if memory construction is highly unreliable; these are framed as necessary caveats to applicability.
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: Those constraints and failure modes are explicitly discussed, investigated via ablations and analysis (graph growth vs LLM quality, AriGraph w/o episodic), and thus the limitations in the theory are already documented in the paper.

aggregated_results.paper_title: A Survey on Large Language Model-Based Game Agents

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: The survey explicitly discusses limitations and failure modes: working-memory capacity and inference-latency trade-offs, unreliable extraction/noisy triplet construction degrading downstream retrieval, and cases where short-term context suffices so long-term memory yields little benefit.
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: The theory includes matching scoped exceptions (little benefit for tasks solvable with short-term context; degraded gains when memory construction is unreliable), framing when hybrid memory is unlikely to help.
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: These constraints and special cases are already present and discussed in the survey, so the limitation content in the theory is already explicit and established by the reviewed literature.

aggregated_results.paper_title: MemInsight: Autonomous Memory Augmentation for LLM Agents

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: The paper explicitly reports limitations: augmentations can be overly generic in ambiguous contexts, performance depends on the backbone LLM quality, occasional failed attribute extraction (small fraction), and some metrics do not uniformly improve; it also states current work is limited to text interactions.
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: The theory lists special cases where hybrid memory gives little benefit (short-term context/trivial mappings) and where unreliable memory construction disrupts benefits (e.g., poor triplet extraction).
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: These constraints closely match the paper's documented limitations (dependence on LLM quality, unreliable augmentations reducing benefit and limited benefit for trivial tasks), so the theory's constraints are already acknowledged in the paper.

aggregated_results.paper_title: Reflexion: Language Agents with Verbal Reinforcement Learning

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: The paper explicitly documents limitations: Reflexion's effectiveness depends on evaluator/self-evaluation quality (and test generation in programming), uses a bounded sliding-window memory that limits long-horizon persistence, can get stuck in local minima (WebShop failure), and suffers if internal tests are flaky or incorrect.
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: The theory likewise acknowledges limits: limited benefit for tasks solvable from short-term context and degraded or disrupted benefits when structured-memory construction (e.g., noisy triplet extraction) is unreliable.
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: These constraint/limitation claims substantially overlap with and are already explicit in the paper (dependence on evaluator quality, memory-window limits, local-minima failure modes), so the paper already establishes the relevant constraints and the theory's similar caveats are not novel with respect to Reflexion.

aggregated_results.paper_title: Learning Dynamic Belief Graphs to Generalize on Text-Based Games

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: The paper explicitly documents limitations and failure modes: learned continuous belief graphs can be dense/noisy and include non-interpretable features; discrete graph updaters are prone to error accumulation; GATA underperforms an agent with full ground-truth graphs (upper bound); and simple short-horizon tasks yield little benefit from structured representations.
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: The theory’s listed constraints (no benefit for trivial short-context games; unreliable memory-construction reduces or disrupts gains) closely match the paper’s observed failure regimes and cautions about noisy or brittle graph construction.
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: These constraints are explicitly observed and discussed in the paper and therefore are not novel relative to the paper.

aggregated_results.paper_title: Large Language Models Are Neurosymbolic Reasoners

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: The paper explicitly documents practical limitations: constrained prompts affect performance, agents forget navigation routes (increasing steps), limited internal/context memory hinders sorting logic, and agents remain error-prone and uncertain in some tasks; it suggests more detailed prompts or improved modules could mitigate these issues.
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: The theory lists special-case constraints: hybrid-memory benefits may be negligible for tasks solvable from short-term context, and benefits diminish if memory construction (e.g., triplet extraction) is highly unreliable.
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: There is overlap between the paper's documented failure modes (forgetting, limited memory capacity, task-dependent prompt efficacy) and the theory's caveats; however, the paper does not analyze these as formal necessary conditions for a hybrid-memory approach nor experimentally probe reliability thresholds for memory construction. Thus the constraint-related observations exist in the paper but only peripherally relative to the hybrid-memory claim.

aggregated_results.paper_title: Graph Constrained Reinforcement Learning for Natural Language Action Spaces

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: The paper documents practical limitations and necessary components: access to valid-action supervision (Jericho), the template action space (vs. full vocabulary decoding), and robust triplet extraction (OpenIE plus custom rules) are important for performance; ablations show removing these degrades results.
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: The theory enumerates special cases where hybrid-memory benefits are reduced: (1) tasks solvable from short-term context may show little benefit and (2) highly unreliable memory construction (e.g., noisy triplet extraction) can disrupt hybrid-memory gains.
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: These cautions echo limitations already discussed or implied in the paper (need for valid-action signals, template necessity, OpenIE extraction issues); the theory's explicit articulation of unreliable memory-construction as a failure mode is consistent with the paper and represents a peripheral formalization rather than a new constraint discovery.

aggregated_results.paper_title: Positive Experience Reflection for Agents in Interactive Text Environments

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: The paper explicitly notes limitations: results are from a single environment (ScienceWorld), reflection gives little benefit on trivial/short-horizon tasks, and effectiveness depends on model capacity (smaller LLMs benefit differently).
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: The theory lists constraints including little benefit for tasks solvable with short-term context and reduced/negative effects when structured-memory construction (e.g., unreliable triplet extraction) is noisy or unreliable.
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: The short-task and model-size sensitivity constraints are present and discussed in the paper; however the specific constraint about failures arising from unreliable structured-memory construction is not evaluated or discussed and therefore is novel relative to this paper (a peripheral but untested caveat).

aggregated_results.paper_title: Behavior Cloned Transformers are Neurosymbolic Reasoners

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: The paper explicitly documents key limitations that constrain applicability: privileged access to exhaustive valid-action lists (valid-action aid), reliance on gold trajectories for behavior cloning, and the DRRN's failure under their setup; it also notes action-space growth when many module actions are injected and dependence on pretrained model priors for some tasks.
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: The theory lists special-case constraints (little benefit for trivially solvable short-horizon games and reduced gains when structured-memory extraction is highly unreliable) that bound when hybrid-memory integration will help.
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: The paper contains overlapping but different constraints; it does not analyze or quantify failure modes arising from noisy structured-memory construction or delineate the boundary between short-term-context tasks and those requiring hybrid memory, so the theory's specific constraints are only partially anticipated by the paper and are not emphasized as contributions.

aggregated_results.paper_title: Reasoning, Memorization, and Fine-Tuning Language Models for Non-Cooperative Games

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: The paper reports concrete limitations: higher token/inference cost from using four agents and back-and-forth communication, compute limits on fine-tuning experiments, reliance on replacing invalid actions with random valid ones during evaluation, and clear performance degradation when summarization or area-selection are removed.
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: The theory enumerates failure regimes and necessary conditions: trivial short-horizon games may show little benefit from hybrid memory, and unreliable structured-memory construction (e.g., noisy triplet extraction) can reduce or disrupt gains from hybrid architectures.
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: The paper documents practical constraints (cost, limited long-term memory handling) but does not empirically analyze sensitivity to unreliable structured-extraction pipelines or define the boundary conditions where hybrid memory fails; those specific failure modes are stated by the theory but are not evaluated in the paper and are therefore only peripherally addressed.

aggregated_results.paper_title: Learning Knowledge Graph-based World Models of Textual Environments

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: The paper documents several limitations and failure modes: extractive approaches over-extract and fail when information is hidden; short-term-context tasks are less likely to benefit from complex world models; label imbalance and unreliable extraction harm valid-action prediction.
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: The theory includes explicit special-case constraints: little benefit when games are solvable from short-term context, and reduced/ disrupted benefits when memory construction (e.g., triplet extraction) is highly unreliable.
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: These constraint statements largely overlap with caveats and failure modes the paper already reports (hidden/latent information, short-horizon tasks, unreliable extraction). The law's constraints re-state and generalize those practical limitations rather than introduce wholly new constraints.

aggregated_results.paper_title: Generative Agents: Interactive Simulacra of Human Behavior

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: The paper documents several limitations and failure modes: retrieval failures and incomplete retrieval, occasional hallucinated embellishments, mis-specified environmental norms causing erratic behavior, computational/cost scalability issues, and vulnerability to prompt- and memory-hacking; it also notes limited evaluation timescale and instruction-tuning biases.
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: The Hybrid Memory Synergy Law explicitly includes constraints: little benefit for tasks solvable with short-term context or trivial mappings, and degraded benefits if structured-memory construction (e.g., triplet extraction or KG building) is highly unreliable or noisy.
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: The paper already reports retrieval and hallucination-related limits and notes scalability and robustness concerns, but it does not specifically study the failure regime where an unreliable structured-memory construction pipeline (noisy triplet/KG extraction) negates the benefit of combining structured and episodic memories; this specific constraint is mentioned by the law but not empirically explored in the paper.

aggregated_results.paper_title: Monte Carlo Planning with Large Language Model for Text-Based Game Agents

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: The paper explicitly notes limitations: truncated in-trial memory can miss distant clues (needle-in-a-haystack), input-window constraints require truncation/compression, unreliable or absent cross-trial memory reduces performance (shown by ablations), and LLM-alone agents can loop without effective exploration strategies.
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: It documents practical failure regimes and cautions that the chosen short in-trial memory window and LLM input constraints limit performance on puzzles requiring distant context; ablations quantify sensitivity to the memory modules and dynamic pruning.
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: These limitations overlap with parts of the theory's special cases (e.g., little benefit in short-horizon games, reduced gains if memory construction is poor), but the paper does not evaluate structured-memory-specific failure modes (e.g., noisy triplet extraction), so the theory's structured-memory constraint is not examined here.

aggregated_results.paper_title: Evaluating and Enhancing LLMs Agent based on Theory of Mind in Guandan (搜蛋): A Multi-Player Cooperative Game under Imperfect Information

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: The paper empirically shows concrete limitations of vanilla LLM-agent approaches in Guandan: (1) severe difficulty processing very long, dynamic valid-action lists (agents preferentially select from first few items), (2) reliance on an external RL-based action recommender to reduce action-space burden, (3) persistent performance gap to a SOTA RL agent (Danzero+) for most models, and (4) sensitivity to player position and to top-k selection hyperparameters (smaller k reduces ToM benefit).
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: The hybrid-memory theory lists special cases where memory benefits are small (short-horizon/trivial mapping games) and warns that unreliable memory extraction reduces benefit. The paper introduces complementary, concrete operational constraints that degrade LLM-agent performance (long action lists, need for tool assistance, limited ToM gain when candidate set is too small).
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: The paper provides explicit empirical constraints relevant to whether memory-like augmentations will help: it documents that without tooling to prune action lists, LLMs fail to exploit planning/ToM effectively — an operational limitation orthogonal to but relevant for assessing hybrid-memory claims. These constraints are empirically explicit in the paper but not previously established for Guandan.

aggregated_results.paper_title: Keep CALM and Explore: Language Models for Action Generation in Text-based Games

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: The paper documents several practical limitations and constraints: CALM confers little benefit if k is too small (misses complex actions), pretraining is essential (random-init model performs poorly), some games receive no benefit, and reducing the action set can in principle omit key actions (they analyze max-in-episode vs learned averages). It also notes dataset/noise issues (ClubFloyd transcripts contain mistakes) and tradeoffs between precision and recall as k varies.
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: The Hybrid Memory Synergy Law includes constraints: hybrid benefits are small for games solvable with short-term context and are degraded if structured-memory construction (e.g., triplet extraction) is highly unreliable.
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: The short-term-context caveat partially overlaps with the paper's empirical observation that some games show little benefit, which the paper treats as a peripheral finding; however, the specific constraint that unreliable structured-memory extraction will disrupt hybrid benefits is not investigated or evidenced in the paper and is a new, plausible limitation not analyzed here.

aggregated_results.paper_title: Pre-trained Language Models as Prior Knowledge for Playing Text-based Games

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: The paper reports heterogeneous gains across games (some games get little or no improvement), TDQN benefits less than DRRN, fine-tuned LM transfer is imperfect, and stronger exploration strategies are still necessary to discover higher-score trajectories; it explicitly notes tasks solvable via short-term context or memorization may not benefit from richer priors.
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: The theory lists concrete constraints: hybrid memory offers little benefit on tasks solvable with short-term context or trivial observation-action mappings, and benefits degrade if structured-memory construction (e.g., noisy triplet extraction) is unreliable.
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: The 'little benefit on short/simple tasks' constraint is supported by the paper's heterogeneous results and is thus an explicit peripheral limitation already visible; however the specific failure mode tied to unreliable structured-memory construction (pipeline noise) is not discussed or evaluated in the paper and is an additional practical constraint introduced by the theory.

aggregated_results.paper_title: Deciphering Digital Detectives: Understanding LLM Behaviors and Capabilities in Multi-Agent Mystery Games

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: The paper explicitly reports some limitations: memoryless agents gain less information about others, gains are small when memory is unnecessary, results vary due to LLM stochasticity, and experiments are confined to a Chinese dataset with reproducibility and cost caveats.
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: The theory states explicit constraints: little benefit in trivial short-horizon tasks and reduced or disrupted gains when structured-memory construction (e.g., triplet extraction) is highly unreliable.
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: While the paper documents that memory brings little benefit when short-term context suffices, it does not evaluate failure modes caused by unreliable structured-memory construction; the theory's explicit failure mode regarding noisy/failed structured-memory is not tested but is a plausible, derivable caution.

aggregated_results.paper_title: Knowledge-enhanced Agents for Interactive Text Games

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: The paper reports explicit failure modes and limits: affordance injection can hurt performance when affordances are irrelevant or noisy (13/40 cases), naively concatenating affordances or using a separate encoder can introduce interference, MCA helps only modestly and can sometimes degrade performance, and benefits depend on affordance quality and task relevance.
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: It identifies concrete negative regimes and engineering constraints (e.g., prefer adding affordances into structured KGs for KG-A2C rather than raw input concatenation) and quantifies when injections backfire across models and tasks.
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: Those practical constraints and failure regimes are newly documented for this set of models and ScienceWorld tasks; they are important empirical findings but are broadly consistent with expected issues (irrelevant/noisy external knowledge causing interference).

aggregated_results.paper_title: A Mixture-of-Experts Approach to Few-Shot Task Transfer in Open-Ended Text Worlds

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: The paper identifies concrete limitations of its MoE design: premature convergence to early-reward experts, expert starvation, degraded performance when relevant experts are removed (adversarial setup), and transient slowdown when many distractor experts are present; it proposes epsilon-greedy sampling and a flattening loss to mitigate these.
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: The theory lists additional constraints specific to hybrid structured+episodic memory (e.g., minimal benefit for tasks solvable with short-term context; benefits reduced if structured-memory extraction is highly unreliable), which are not empirically measured in the paper.
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: While the paper documents failure regimes for the MoE architecture, the particular constraints tied to unreliable triplet extraction or to interaction effects between structured and episodic memory are not analyzed here and thus represent new, untested limitations relative to the paper.

aggregated_results.paper_title: Thespian: Multi-Character Text Role-Playing Game Agents

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: The paper documents specific limitations and failure modes of its architecture and ablations: attention over prompts or over softmaxed probabilities failed in ablations, softmaxing before fusion degraded performance, random prompts produce poor behavior, unfrozen retraining causes catastrophic forgetting, and pretraining biases (map layout) influence attention weights.
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: The theory lists constraints for the hybrid-memory claim: limited benefit in tasks solvable with short-term context and degraded gains when memory construction (e.g., triplet extraction) is unreliable.
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: Some practical constraints (catastrophic forgetting, attention-design sensitivity, environment bias) are explicitly observed in the paper, but the specific constraints about unreliable structured/episodic memory construction and task classes with negligible benefit are not studied here and thus are not established by this paper; they are plausible but not demonstrated.

aggregated_results.paper_title: diff History for Neural Language Agents

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: The paper explicitly lists limitations: diff requires observations expressible in structured textual form, may hurt or increase token count in narrative/unstructured observations, and therefore may not help tasks lacking inherent observational structure; it also notes the assumption that observations/actions are available as text.
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: The theory adds constraints/special cases: negligible benefit for tasks solvable from short-term context and reduced/disrupted benefits when structured-memory construction (e.g., triplet extraction) is highly unreliable or noisy.
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: The paper already documents environment-structure-related failure modes and the need for textual observations; the specific caveat that noisy/ unreliable construction of explicit structured memory will diminish hybrid-memory benefits is not empirically analyzed in the paper but follows plausibly from its observations about reliance on structured signal.

aggregated_results.paper_title: Language Models are Few-Shot Butlers

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: The paper documents practical limitations: iterated action modeling requires an initial non-zero success rate (hence a few demonstrations to bootstrap), contexts are limited by a sliding window (max sequence length), only successful trajectories are used for fine-tuning, and decoding/hyperparameter choices affect outcomes.
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: The theory lists special-case constraints such as negligible benefit on tasks solvable with short-term context and degraded or disrupted gains when structured-memory construction (e.g., triplet extraction) is highly unreliable or noisy.
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: While the paper reports operational constraints for IAM and context-size limits, it does not analyze failure modes specific to constructing or using explicit structured memory (e.g., extraction noise, incorrect triplets), so the structured-memory-specific limitations in the theory are not present in the paper.

aggregated_results.paper_title: RAP: Retrieval-Augmented Planning with Contextual Memory for Multimodal LLM Agents

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: The paper acknowledges environment-dependent design choices (adaptive component weights), shows retrieval can add noise if full trajectories are used (hence windowing), and reports varying gains by task complexity; it provides ablations on retrieval modes and embeddings but lacks analysis of structured-memory construction failures.
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: The theory explicitly states constraints: hybrid memory yields little benefit in trivial short-horizon tasks, and benefits are reduced or disrupted if structured-memory construction (e.g., triplet extraction) is highly unreliable or noisy.
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: The specific limitation concerning the fragility of constructed structured/relational memories and their failure modes is not analyzed or evidenced in the paper and therefore is a new, untested constraint relative to RAP's results.

generalization_scope_expansion.what_is_known: The AriGraph paper explicitly demonstrates and validates hybrid structured (knowledge-graph) + episodic memory integration across multiple interactive text-game families (Treasure Hunt, Cleaning, Cooking, constrained NetHack) and static multi-hop QA benchmarks (MuSiQue, HotpotQA), reporting robust cross-domain performance, scaling behavior, and treating this cross-domain generalization as a primary contribution. One additional paper (Knowledge-enhanced Agents) provides explicit but peripheral multi-task evaluation across ScienceWorld with both RL and LM paradigms.
generalization_scope_expansion.what_introduced: The theory statement claims that hybrid structured+episodic memory integration enables superior normalized performance, reduced error rates, and greater sample efficiency and generalization for LLM agents specifically across text-based games with partial observability, combinatorial action spaces, and complex state transitions (e.g., TextWorld, NetHack, general text-based tasks requiring multi-step planning, navigation, and long-horizon instruction following), compared to single-memory or unstructured approaches.
generalization_scope_expansion.what_novel: The scope expansion is not novel: AriGraph has already explicitly demonstrated, evaluated, and reported the claimed cross-domain applicability of hybrid structured+episodic memory for LLM agents across representative target environments (interactive text games including constrained NetHack, plus reasoning benchmarks), establishing this generalization as known work. While 21 other papers either suggest the scope would be derivable or genuinely new relative to their more limited experimental domains, this does not override the fact that one paper has already explicitly established and validated the core scope claim as a recognized contribution.

Multiple other papers (9 total) provide partial evidence or conceptual groundwork suggesting the plausibility of such scope expansion through demonstrations in related settings (episodic reflection in Reflexion, structured memory in various text-game agents, diff-based episodic encoding in NetHack), though without explicitly testing the full hybrid integration claim across the stated scope.

While 12 papers suggest this scope would be genuinely new relative to their limited domains, this does not override the fact that the full scope has been explicitly established in prior published work. The theory's scope claim is therefore an explicit restatement rather than a novel expansion.
