theory_law_dict.theory_name: Hybrid Structured and Episodic Memory Enables Robust, Generalizable LLM Agent Performance in Text Games
theory_law_dict.theory_description: Language model agents for text-based games achieve superior robustness, sample efficiency, and generalization when augmented with hybrid memory systems that combine explicit structured (e.g., knowledge-graph or belief-graph) state representations and episodic (trajectory or reflection-based) memory. Structured memory provides compact, relational representations that support multi-hop reasoning, navigation, and action-space pruning, while episodic memory enables recall of detailed instructions, procedures, and successful/failed experiences. The synergistic integration and principled retrieval of both kinds of memories enables agents to overcome both partial observability and long-horizon credit assignment bottlenecks, outperforming agents with either memory alone, with unstructured memory (e.g., full text history), or with naive retrieval/summarization.
theory_law_dict.theory_statement: When both structured (e.g., knowledge-graph/semantic) and episodic (e.g., observation/trajectory/reflective) memory are available and purposefully integrated and retrieved into agent working memory, LLM-based agents achieve higher normalized/game performance, reduced error rates, and greater sample efficiency and generalization compared to agents using only one memory type or only unstructured full-history/retrieval.
theory_law_dict.domain_scope: Applies to LLM agents in text-based games with partial observability, combinatorial action spaces, and complex state transitions, especially in environments requiring multi-step planning, navigation, and long-term instruction following (e.g., TextWorld, NetHack, general text-based tasks)
special_cases: Games solvable via short-term context or trivial observation-to-action mapping may show little benefit.
If memory construction (e.g., triplet extraction) is highly unreliable, benefits are reduced/disrupted.

aggregated_results.paper_title: AriGraph: Learning Knowledge Graph World Models with Episodic Memory for LLM Agents

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: The paper evaluates AriGraph across multiple interactive text-game families (Treasure Hunt, Cleaning, Cooking), a constrained NetHack setting, and adapts the architecture to static multi-hop QA benchmarks (MuSiQue, HotpotQA), reporting robust performance and scaling behavior.
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: The theory states the hybrid-memory advantage holds broadly for LLM agents in partially observable, combinatorial-action, long-horizon text-game domains (e.g., TextWorld, NetHack) and in tasks requiring multi-step planning and multi-hop reasoning.
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: The paper explicitly demonstrates cross-domain applicability and provides evidence that the approach generalizes beyond a single game family (interactive games and QA), so the scope expansion claimed by the theory is supported and already present in the paper.

aggregated_results.paper_title: Knowledge-enhanced Agents for Interactive Text Games

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: Previous KG- and memory-based techniques were evaluated in particular agents or environments; large-scale multi-task empirical evaluation across diverse ScienceWorld tasks and both RL and LM agent classes was lacking.
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: The paper extends evaluation of affordance and episodic-memory injection to ten heterogeneous ScienceWorld tasks and to both RL (online) and LM (offline) paradigms, showing where injections transfer and where they fail.
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: This is an empirical expansion of scope (more tasks, multiple agent families, multiple injection strategies) rather than a theoretical generalization; it documents the transferability and limits of these injections across settings.

aggregated_results.paper_title: A Survey on Large Language Model-Based Game Agents

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: The survey maps memory and reasoning requirements across multiple game genres (action, adventure, sandbox, strategy, simulation, role-playing) and discusses how memory designs address genre-specific demands (e.g., stateful world modeling in adventure games, low-latency trade-offs in action games).
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: The theory claims that hybrid structured+episodic memory integration produces robust, sample-efficient, and generalizable gains specifically for LLM agents in partially observable, combinatorial-action, long-horizon text-game-like domains (e.g., TextWorld, NetHack, other text-based tasks).
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: The paper supplies cross-domain groundwork and genre mappings but does not empirically validate that hybrid memory integration consistently generalizes across the full scope of these domains; the explicit scope-expansion claim (uniform hybrid benefit across specified game classes) is therefore not established in the survey and remains a derived generalization.

aggregated_results.paper_title: Reasoning, Memorization, and Fine-Tuning Language Models for Non-Cooperative Games

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: The paper validates its method on a newly defined non-cooperative game (Goedendag) across two board sizes (4x4, 10x10), reports cross-board fine-tuning gains, and argues the approach could extend to other game types; empirical claims are constrained to this game and configuration.
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: The theory claims a broader scope: hybrid structured+episodic memory architectures will improve robustness, sample efficiency, and generalization for LLM agents across a wide class of text-based games with partial observability, combinatorial actions, and long-horizon planning (e.g., TextWorld, NetHack).
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: Extending the paper's demonstrated advantages of compact representations and task decomposition to the broad class of environments and to hybrid memory architectures is not tested here; while the extension is a plausible generalization, it is not demonstrated and remains an unstated derivation from the paper's limited empirical scope.

aggregated_results.paper_title: Reflexion: Language Agents with Verbal Reinforcement Learning

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: Reflexion demonstrates cross-task generalization of episodic verbal reinforcement across decision-making, reasoning, and programming tasks and evaluates variations in memory size, evaluator types, and base model strength.
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: The theory extends the claim to a broader scope, asserting that combining structured semantic/graph memories with episodic reflections yields robustness, sample efficiency, and generalization in harder, partial-observability, combinatorial-action domains (e.g., NetHack-like environments) beyond the textual episodic traces tested.
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: Extending Reflexion's episodic-memory results to explicit structured+episodic benefits across broader domains is not demonstrated in the paper; while plausible from background literature, this extension is not tested here and is therefore a derivable-but-unstated generalization relative to the paper.

aggregated_results.paper_title: Learning Knowledge Graph-based World Models of Textual Environments

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: The paper's domain is LLM/world-modeling in text-based games with partial observability and large action vocabularies; experiments are on JerichoWorld games and the claims are about improved KG and action prediction generalization to never-before-seen textual worlds within that benchmark.
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: The theory extends the claim to a broader scope: hybrid structured+episodic memory will increase robustness, sample efficiency, and generalization of LLM agents across text-game domains (including more complex environments like NetHack and long-horizon planning tasks) compared to single-memory or unstructured-history approaches.
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: While the paper shows structured-memory benefits within JerichoWorld prediction tasks, it does not evaluate hybrid memory or downstream gameplay performance or larger/different environments; the theory's broader scope (gameplay-level normalized performance, NetHack-scale environments, and sample efficiency claims) is not established by this paper.

aggregated_results.paper_title: Generative Agents: Interactive Simulacra of Human Behavior

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: The authors demonstrate the architecture in a visual/simulated Smallville sandbox with 25 agents and argue qualitatively that the memory+reflection+planning pattern could transfer to other interactive settings; they discuss but do not empirically test extensions to other domains.
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: The Hybrid Memory Synergy Law explicitly scopes the claim to LLM agents in text-based games with partial observability, combinatorial action spaces, and complex state transitions (e.g., TextWorld, NetHack), asserting that a hybrid structured+episodic memory yields robustness, sample efficiency, and generalization in those domains.
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: Applying the paper's Smallville results to complex text-adventure / partially observable combinatorial-action domains and claiming improved sample efficiency and generalization is a substantive scope expansion not evaluated in this paper; the paper does not provide experiments or metrics in those target domains to support that generalization.

aggregated_results.paper_title: Monte Carlo Planning with Large Language Model for Text-Based Game Agents

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: The paper demonstrates MC-DML's effectiveness across a diverse set of nine Jericho games, including long-horizon and sparse-reward tasks, indicating generalization across multiple games within the text-game POMDP domain.
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: It proposes that dynamic memory-guided LLM priors can improve initial planning across varied, hard textual games without a planning-then-learning cycle, and provides empirical support for that claim within the Jericho benchmark.
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: The theory extends this scope further (e.g., TextWorld, NetHack, and claims about robustness to partial observability and combinatorial action spaces when combining structured and episodic memory); those broader domain generalizations and the structured+episodic scope expansion are not evaluated in the paper and therefore remain untested here.

aggregated_results.paper_title: diff History for Neural Language Agents

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: Empirically, the paper demonstrates diff history benefits in two procedurally-generated text environments of different dimensionality (BabyAI-Text and NetHack), reports scaling with more data in NetHack, and characterizes when diff compresses versus expands token counts.
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: The theory generalizes to a broader architectural claim: hybrid structured+episodic memory systems will improve robustness, sample efficiency, and generalization for LLM agents across partially-observable, combinatorial, long-horizon text-game domains (e.g., TextWorld, NetHack) and will outperform agents with only one memory type or naive full-history retrieval.
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: Extending the paper's result (that a specific structured episodic encoding helps) to the broader architectural generalization that arbitrary hybrid structured+episodic memory systems will systematically outperform single-memory alternatives is not tested; the paper's experiments make the hybrid hypothesis plausible but do not establish it across memory architectures or domains.

aggregated_results.paper_title: Evaluating and Enhancing LLMs Agent based on Theory of Mind in Guandan (搜蛋): A Multi-Player Cooperative Game under Imperfect Information

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: The paper demonstrates that prompting-based Theory-of-Mind (zero/first/second-order) planning and an external RL-based action recommender enable off-the-shelf LLMs to operate in a complex, non-English, multi-player imperfect-information card game (Guandan). It shows consistent performance improvements across multiple LLM families and model sizes when ToM planning and the external tool are applied. The work explicitly applies modular LLM-agent components (State Interpreter, ToM Planner, Plan Evaluator) to a realistic card-game domain.
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: The theory statement generalizes the benefit of hybrid structured+episodic memory to text-game LLM agents and claims improved robustness, sample efficiency and generalization in partial observability and long-horizon planning tasks. It scopes that claim to environments like TextWorld, NetHack and text-based tasks requiring multi-step planning and navigation.
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: The paper does generalize prior LLM planning/ToM work to a new, culturally-specific, multi-agent imperfect-information card game and to non-English settings; however it does not implement or evaluate hybrid structured+episodic memory. Therefore the paper provides partial, domain-level generalization evidence for LLM-agent augmentation (ToM + external tool) but not for the specific hybrid-memory claim.

aggregated_results.paper_title: RAP: Retrieval-Augmented Planning with Contextual Memory for Multimodal LLM Agents

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: RAP generalizes retrieval-augmented episodic memory beyond pure text tasks into multimodal embodied benchmarks and across multiple LLM/VLM backbones, and demonstrates cross-model transfer of stored experiences (memories built with one model can benefit another).
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: The theory claims a broader generalization: that hybrid structured+episodic memory yields robust, sample-efficient, and generalizable performance specifically in partially observable, combinatorial-action, long-horizon text-game domains (e.g., TextWorld, NetHack) and other complex sequential decision settings.
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: The paper validates multimodal and textual generalization for episodic retrieval but does not test the additional axis of generalization to hybrid structured+episodic systems nor to the more extreme, combinatorial domains named; that further scope-expansion is not demonstrated in the paper.

aggregated_results.paper_title: Pre-trained Language Models as Prior Knowledge for Playing Text-based Games

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: The paper demonstrates LM-augmentation across 14 Jericho games, reports improved sample efficiency and limited transfer (Zork1→Zork3), and emphasizes applicability to IF/text-based game benchmarks that are partially observable with combinatorial action spaces.
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: The theory expands scope by claiming the hybrid structured+episodic memory principle will produce robust, generalizable gains for LLM agents across a broader class of challenging text environments (e.g., TextWorld, NetHack, long-horizon planning and navigation tasks) beyond LM-only augmentation.
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: The paper's experiments show LM benefits within Jericho, but they do not test hybrid structured+episodic architectures nor domains like NetHack; extending the claim to those broader settings is an extrapolation not validated in the paper and therefore constitutes a novel scope expansion.

aggregated_results.paper_title: Large Language Models Are Neurosymbolic Reasoners

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: The paper demonstrates that zero-shot LLM agents with symbolic modules generalize across four symbolic tasks (Arithmetic, MapReader, Sorting, TWC) within TextWorld-derived benchmarks and across two model families (GPT-3.5, GPT-4), showing the prompting+module approach transfers within this benchmark suite.
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: The Hybrid Memory Synergy Law extends scope by claiming that hybrid structured+episodic memory augmentation will improve robustness, sample efficiency, and generalization for LLM agents broadly in partially observable, combinatorial, long-horizon text-game domains (e.g., TextWorld, NetHack, and other complex text-based tasks).
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: The paper does not evaluate hybrid memory across these broader domains or scales; its experiments are limited to symbolic-module tool use on TextWorldExpress tasks, so the broader scope claim (other environments, more complex partial observability or combinatorial-action domains) is not supported or tested here.

aggregated_results.paper_title: Graph Constrained Reinforcement Learning for Natural Language Action Spaces

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: The paper demonstrates cross-game generality of the KG+template approach by evaluating on 28 diverse human-authored IF games and describes relaxing graph-extraction rules to improve applicability across genres.
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: The theory expands scope to (a) explicitly hybrid structured+episodic memory systems, (b) application to LLM-based decision architectures (rather than the GRU/GAT policies used here), and (c) broader domains requiring longer-horizon planning (e.g., NetHack, TextWorld variants).
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: Extending the KG+template insights to include episodic-memory integration and to LLM-driven agents and additional domains is not demonstrated in the paper; these scope expansions are additional claims beyond the paper's experiments and thus novel relative to it.

aggregated_results.paper_title: Deciphering Digital Detectives: Understanding LLM Behaviors and Capabilities in Multi-Agent Mystery Games

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: The paper demonstrates benefits of MR+SR+SV in one domain (Chinese Jubensha multi-agent detective games) and reports limitations and domain-specific caveats (language specificity, stochasticity, cost), but provides no cross-environment empirical validation.
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: The theory generalizes the claim to a broader class of LLM agent text-game domains (e.g., TextWorld, NetHack) and to settings with partial observability, combinatorial actions, and long-horizon planning, asserting superiority of a hybrid structured+episodic memory over single/unstructured memories.
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: Extending the paper's single-domain experimental findings to a broad cross-environment claim about structured+episodic hybrids is not demonstrated or analyzed in the paper and therefore represents a novel generalization beyond the presented evidence.

aggregated_results.paper_title: MemInsight: Autonomous Memory Augmentation for LLM Agents

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: MemInsight's experiments and empirical claims are confined to text-dialogue tasks (LLM-REDIAL, LoCoMo) — QA, recommendation, and summarization — and the authors note the implementation is text-only and could be extended to multimodal inputs in future work.
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: The theory extends the claimed benefits to a broader and more challenging domain: LLM agents in text-based games with partial observability, combinatorial action spaces, and long-horizon planning (e.g., TextWorld, NetHack), asserting improved robustness and sample efficiency there.
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: This cross-domain extrapolation (from dialogue benchmarks to complex interactive text-game environments) is a substantive scope expansion not supported by experiments or analysis in the paper, so it represents a genuine generalization beyond the presented evidence.

aggregated_results.paper_title: Positive Experience Reflection for Agents in Interactive Text Environments

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: The paper demonstrates Sweet&Sour on ScienceWorld tasks and across three LLM sizes, and claims applicability to interactive text environments that use self-reflection and managed memory.
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: The theory asserts a broader scope: hybrid structured+episodic memory improves robustness, sample efficiency, and generalization across partially observable, combinatorial-action text-game domains (e.g., TextWorld, NetHack) and outperforms single-memory or unstructured full-history retrieval.
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: While the paper generalizes episodic/managed-reflection benefits within one benchmark and multiple LLMs, it provides no experiments or analysis showing that adding structured relational memory yields the claimed cross-domain/generalization benefits; that expanded hybrid-scope claim is not supported here.

aggregated_results.paper_title: Behavior Cloned Transformers are Neurosymbolic Reasoners

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: Empirically, the paper shows that action-injection generalizes across four distinct text-game tasks and is implementable with different symbolic modules and a behavior-cloned T5 model, but experiments are restricted to gold-trajectory supervision, the valid-action aid, and specific benchmarks.
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: The theory claims broader scope: that hybrid structured+episodic memory integration improves robustness, sample efficiency, and generalization for LLM agents across text games with partial observability, combinatorial action spaces, and long-horizon planning (e.g., TextWorld, NetHack), surpassing single-memory or unstructured-history approaches.
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: Although the paper supports that structured symbolic augmentation helps in several benchmarks, it does not evaluate episodic-memory mechanisms, principled joint-retrieval policies, or varied LLM architectures and environments at the scale claimed by the theory, so that scope expansion is not established here.

aggregated_results.paper_title: A Mixture-of-Experts Approach to Few-Shot Task Transfer in Open-Ended Text Worlds

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: The paper demonstrates results in a LIGHT-derived open-ended text-role environment using KG-A2C experts and evaluates blended/partial-blend role transfer; it does not test LLM-based agents, NetHack, TextWorld, or explicit episodic-memory modules.
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: The theory claims the hybrid structured+episodic memory principle generalizes to LLM-based agents across partially-observable, combinatorial-action text games and yields superior robustness, sample efficiency, and generalization over single-memory or naive full-history retrieval approaches.
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: Extending the paper's MoE+KG results to LLM-based agents and to a broad class of text-game domains (and to explicit episodic memory mechanisms) is not demonstrated or evaluated here; that scope expansion is a substantive, novel claim beyond the paper's experiments.

aggregated_results.paper_title: Language Models are Few-Shot Butlers

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: Empirically, the paper demonstrates in-distribution and out-of-distribution generalization within ALFWorld, including robustness to human-annotated goals, and quantifies sample-efficiency gains from iterated action modeling; experiments are scoped to ALFWorld and use sliding context windows and specific decoding/hyperparameter settings.
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: The theory statement explicitly claims broader scope: that hybrid structured+episodic memory benefits apply to LLM agents across partially-observable, combinatorial, long-horizon text games (e.g., TextWorld, NetHack) and enable overcoming partial observability and long-horizon credit assignment more generally.
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: Cross-environment generalization and the claim that hybrid memory architectures deliver similar benefits across diverse text-game domains (and scale beyond ALFWorld) are not demonstrated in the paper, nor is structured memory evaluated; thus the scope expansion in the theory is not supported by this work.

aggregated_results.paper_title: Thespian: Multi-Character Text Role-Playing Game Agents

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: The paper's experiments and claims are scoped to the LIGHT text role-playing environment and variants of that map, and the method is evaluated for multi-character learning and few-shot adaptation within that single environment family.
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: The theory asserts broader applicability: that hybrid structured+episodic memory improves robustness, sample efficiency, and generalization for LLM-based agents across a range of text-game domains (e.g., TextWorld, NetHack) and in tasks requiring multi-step planning and long-horizon instruction following.
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: Those broader domain and modality generalizations are not evaluated in the paper; extending the empirical claim to other text-game platforms and to explicit episodic memory components is a novel expansion beyond this work.

aggregated_results.paper_title: Keep CALM and Explore: Language Models for Action Generation in Text-based Games

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: This work demonstrates that a single LM (CALM) trained on diverse human transcripts generalizes to unseen Jericho games and that the LM+DRRN pipeline can be applied across many man-made text games with partial observability and large action spaces; the paper also explores sensitivity to k and pretraining.
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: The Hybrid Memory Synergy Law explicitly generalizes the claim to a broader class of LLM agents and tasks: that hybrid structured+episodic memory integration improves robustness, sample efficiency, and generalization across text-based games requiring multi-step planning, navigation, and long-horizon instruction following (e.g., TextWorld, NetHack).
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: Extending the empirical claim from LM-only candidate generation (paper) to a broad cross-methods claim about hybrid memory systems and their advantages across a wider set of environments and failure modes is not supported or addressed in the paper; the hybrid scope expansion goes beyond the experiments and domains evaluated.

aggregated_results.paper_title: Learning Dynamic Belief Graphs to Generalize on Text-Based Games

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: The paper demonstrates distributional generalization within TextWorld: an agent trained on a set of generated games generalizes to unseen games across multiple difficulty levels and mixed-level testing, showing structured belief graphs improve cross-instance generalization in this environment family.
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: The theory claims a broader scope: that hybrid structured+episodic memory will enable robust, sample-efficient, and generalizable performance for LLM-based agents across many text-game domains (e.g., TextWorld and NetHack) and more generally in long-horizon, partially observable language-mediated tasks.
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: Extending the TextWorld-specific empirical result to other environments and to a general statement about hybrid memory for LLM agents is not supported by the paper’s experiments; that scope expansion is a substantive, new claim beyond the paper’s demonstrated domain.

explanatory_mechanistic.what_is_known: One paper (AriGraph, 2024) provides a complete, explicit implementation and validation of the dual-memory mechanistic account: structured KG memory (V_s, E_s for semantic triplets) combined with episodic memory (V_e, E_e for observation traces), integrated via concrete two-stage retrieval algorithms, demonstrating that their principled integration causally produces superior performance in text-based games. One earlier paper (Knowledge-enhanced Agents) describes mechanistic roles for structured KGs and episodic traces but treats these as applications of existing known mechanisms (affordances, MCA) rather than highlighting their synergy as a novel discovery. A survey paper and several theoretical works describe the separate functional contributions of each memory type and their consolidation/retrieval mechanisms, making the integrated causal claim derivable but not explicitly stated as a validated principle.
explanatory_mechanistic.what_introduced: The theory articulates a specific causal decomposition asserting that structured memory enables compact relational representations supporting multi-hop reasoning, navigation, and action-space pruning, while episodic memory preserves detailed procedural/contextual traces aiding long-horizon credit assignment and instruction following; crucially, it claims that principled joint retrieval of both into working memory synergistically resolves partial observability and long-horizon bottlenecks. This complete mechanistic account—specifying distinct causal roles, their complementary functions, and the retrieval-mediated synergy—is explicitly instantiated and empirically validated in AriGraph (2024), anticipated peripherally in one prior paper, and derivable from survey/theoretical syntheses, but was not proposed or tested in the majority of work.
explanatory_mechanistic.what_novel: The complete, validated mechanistic account is explicit and established as of AriGraph (2024), which provides full empirical support for the theory's causal claims. However, relative to the broader field, this mechanistic account was genuinely novel: 68% of papers (15/22) do not propose, implement, or validate this dual-memory causal mechanism, instead offering alternative explanations (LM priors, tool use, compression, reflection-only, graph-only mechanisms, or multi-agent decomposition). An additional 23% (5/22) found the mechanism derivable but unstated, meaning it could be inferred from their components but was not explicitly formulated or tested. Only one paper beyond AriGraph treated elements of this mechanism as known (explicit_peripheral). The mechanistic novelty is therefore temporally concentrated: it represents an explicit, validated contribution as of 2024, but was largely absent or only implicit in prior literature.

One earlier paper (Knowledge-enhanced Agents) describes mechanistic roles for structured and episodic components but treats these as applications of known mechanisms rather than novel discoveries.

This mechanistic account is explicitly instantiated and validated in AriGraph, partially anticipated in one other paper, derivable from several survey/theoretical papers, but absent from the majority (15/22) of papers which either implement only one memory type or provide alternative mechanistic explanations.

theory_law_dict.theory_name: Hybrid Structured and Episodic Memory Enables Robust, Generalizable LLM Agent Performance in Text Games
theory_law_dict.theory_description: Language model agents for text-based games achieve superior robustness, sample efficiency, and generalization when augmented with hybrid memory systems that combine explicit structured (e.g., knowledge-graph or belief-graph) state representations and episodic (trajectory or reflection-based) memory. Structured memory provides compact, relational representations that support multi-hop reasoning, navigation, and action-space pruning, while episodic memory enables recall of detailed instructions, procedures, and successful/failed experiences. The synergistic integration and principled retrieval of both kinds of memories enables agents to overcome both partial observability and long-horizon credit assignment bottlenecks, outperforming agents with either memory alone, with unstructured memory (e.g., full text history), or with naive retrieval/summarization.
theory_law_dict.theory_statement: When both structured (e.g., knowledge-graph/semantic) and episodic (e.g., observation/trajectory/reflective) memory are available and purposefully integrated and retrieved into agent working memory, LLM-based agents achieve higher normalized/game performance, reduced error rates, and greater sample efficiency and generalization compared to agents using only one memory type or only unstructured full-history/retrieval.
theory_law_dict.domain_scope: Applies to LLM agents in text-based games with partial observability, combinatorial action spaces, and complex state transitions, especially in environments requiring multi-step planning, navigation, and long-term instruction following (e.g., TextWorld, NetHack, general text-based tasks)
special_cases: Games solvable via short-term context or trivial observation-to-action mapping may show little benefit.
If memory construction (e.g., triplet extraction) is highly unreliable, benefits are reduced/disrupted.

aggregated_results.paper_title: AriGraph: Learning Knowledge Graph World Models with Episodic Memory for LLM Agents

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: The paper provides a concrete mechanistic account: semantic triplet extraction builds a KG (V_s,E_s), episodic vertices store raw observations (V_e) and episodic edges link triplets from the same timestep (E_e); retrieval is a two-stage semantic-then-episodic search with defined scoring heuristics, and planning/decision modules consume retrieved contents in working memory.
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: The theory attributes performance gains to complementary mechanisms: compact relational representations support multi-hop reasoning, navigation and action pruning, while episodic memory preserves detailed procedural/contextual traces that aid long-horizon credit and instruction following, and their principled joint retrieval enables synergy.
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: Those causal/mechanistic elements (graph structure, episodic links, retrieval algorithms, and their roles in planning/decision making) are explicitly proposed, implemented, and used to explain empirical gains in the paper; the theory's mechanistic claims are therefore present and instantiated.

aggregated_results.paper_title: Knowledge-enhanced Agents for Interactive Text Games

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: The paper attributes improvements to mechanisms such as action-space pruning via affordances, richer relational state representations via KGs, and reinforcement of successful decisions via MCA; these mechanistic rationales are described and connected to implementation choices (KG triples, separate encoders, GRU/MCA windows).
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: These mechanistic accounts are instantiated in the architectures (e.g., affordances added to KG vs. concatenated inputs; MCA encoded with GRU or appended text) and used to interpret per-model and per-task outcomes.
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: The explanations are plausible and align with existing intuitions about KGs and episodic memory; the paper does not propose a fundamentally new causal mechanism beyond applying known mechanisms to these injection designs and empirically verifying their impact.

aggregated_results.paper_title: A Survey on Large Language Model-Based Game Agents

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: The survey explicates mechanistic roles: structured memories (graphs, triplets, keys) support relational/multi-hop retrieval and compact state representations, while episodic memories (trajectories, reflections, summaries) capture ordered procedural experience, success/failure exemplars, and support reflective improvement; it also details consolidation, structuring, and retrieval mechanisms that mediate memory utility.
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: The theory offers a causal account claiming synergy: structured memory provides compact relational scaffolding for multi-step reasoning and action pruning, episodic memory provides detailed procedural recall and credit-assignment signals, and principled joint retrieval into working memory causally overcomes partial observability and long-horizon credit-assignment bottlenecks.
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: The survey already describes the separate functional contributions and retrieval/consolidation mechanisms, but it does not explicitly formulate or empirically validate the specific causal synergy claim (that the combined mechanism reliably resolves both partial observability and long-horizon credit assignment across tasks); thus the integrated causal claim is an inferred synthesis rather than an explicit established mechanism in the paper.

aggregated_results.paper_title: A Mixture-of-Experts Approach to Few-Shot Task Transfer in Open-Ended Text Worlds

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: The paper provides mechanistic components for its results within the MoE: value seeding (multiplying expert action distributions by critic-predicted returns) biases attention toward experts with high expected return, an attention network projects observations and expert logits to select experts, and epsilon-greedy plus an entropy-like loss mitigate premature convergence and expert starvation; KG-A2C provides a persistent, structured KG state representation for each expert.
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: The submitted theory statement attributes the performance gains to a causal synergy between two distinct memory systems—structured relational memory and episodic/trajectory memory—when purposefully integrated into working memory; the paper does not implement or test an episodic-memory substrate or retrieval mechanism and thus does not present or validate this specific causal account.
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: The hybrid structured+episodic causal mechanism claimed by the theory is not proposed, instrumented, or experimentally evaluated in the paper; the paper's mechanistic explanations concern attention/value-seeding in an MoE and KG-based persistent memory only.

aggregated_results.paper_title: Reasoning, Memorization, and Fine-Tuning Language Models for Non-Cooperative Games

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: The paper offers mechanistic explanations for its empirical gains: task decomposition reduces per-agent memory and cognitive load, summarization produces compact, consistent text-based state representations, area selection constrains the exponential state/action space (linear memory growth), and action validation reduces rule violations; fine-tuning via ranked self-play improves task-specific behavior.
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: The theory provides a different mechanistic account: structured memory enables multi-hop relational reasoning, navigation, and action-space pruning while episodic memory stores detailed trajectories, successes/failures and supports credit-assignment and recall; crucially, the retrieval policy integrating both into working memory resolves partial observability and long-horizon credit assignment.
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: The paper's mechanisms overlap with the structured-memory side (compact representations, pruning the search), but it does not define or test an episodic-memory mechanism nor the specific causal interaction (synergy) between structured and episodic stores. The hybrid causal account therefore is not articulated or experimentally validated in the paper and would need to be inferred or developed from multiple sources.

aggregated_results.paper_title: Monte Carlo Planning with Large Language Model for Text-Based Game Agents

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: The paper provides a mechanistic account for the observed improvement: failed simulated trajectories are summarized by the LLM into short textual reflections stored as cross-trial memory, and these reflections, together with an in-trial trajectory window, modulate the LLM prior over actions during subsequent MCTS simulations, thereby biasing exploration away from failure-prone high-immediate-reward actions and improving value estimates.
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: It operationalizes a retrieval-and-reprompt mechanism where LLM-generated reflections are appended to memory and explicitly used in prompts to produce adjusted action probability priors inside PUCT-style selection.
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: This paper supplies a concrete, tested mechanistic pathway for episodic/reflection-driven adjustment of LLM priors in planning, but it does not introduce or evaluate the mechanistic role of structured relational (knowledge-graph) memory nor the interaction mechanics between structured representations and episodic reflections described in the theory.

aggregated_results.paper_title: Learning Dynamic Belief Graphs to Generalize on Text-Based Games

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: The paper provides a mechanistic account for why graph beliefs help: belief graphs encode entities and relations (supporting relational/multi-hop reasoning), the recurrent graph-updater accumulates temporal information to mitigate partial observability, and the graph-encoder + bi-attention aggregator guide action selection; self-supervised pretraining (OG and COC) is used to shape graph semantics. These components and their roles are described and ablated.
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: The submitted theory claims an additional mechanism: a distinct episodic memory (trajectory/experience/reflective store) integrated with structured KG-like memory and principled retrieval yields synergistic benefits (improved long-horizon recall, credit assignment, and robustness).
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: The paper does not implement or evaluate a separate episodic-memory module nor the explicit structured–episodic synergy described in the theory; the hybrid mechanistic claim therefore extends beyond the paper’s implemented mechanisms and results.

aggregated_results.paper_title: Large Language Models Are Neurosymbolic Reasoners

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: The paper offers mechanistic commentary at a high level: LLM agents leverage pretraining-derived patterns and invoke external symbolic modules as tools via prompting, and observed failures are partially explained by limited in-context/memory capacity (e.g., forgetting routes) and prompt design choices. The manuscript does not formalize internal memory architectures or retrieval mechanisms beyond prompting and tool invocation.
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: The theory proposes a mechanistic decomposition in which structured memory provides compact relational representations (supporting multi-hop reasoning, navigation, and action pruning) while episodic memory provides detailed procedural and outcome traces, and their principled retrieval into working memory resolves partial observability and long-horizon credit assignment.
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: That specific causal account (how structured and episodic memories interact, what retrieval policies are required, and how this resolves partial observability/credit-assignment) is not articulated, modeled, or empirically tested in the paper; the paper instead focuses on tool-use via prompts without implementing or analyzing hybrid memory mechanisms.

aggregated_results.paper_title: Graph Constrained Reinforcement Learning for Natural Language Action Spaces

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: The paper provides mechanistic explanations for how the knowledge graph helps: it yields a compact relational state representation, is embedded via GATs for multi-hop reasoning, and its graph mask prunes the combinatorial template action space to focus generation on actionable objects, which together improve exploration and policy learning.
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: The theory proposes a mechanistic account in which structured memory supplies compact relational representations for multi-hop reasoning and action-space pruning while episodic memory supplies detailed procedural/trajectory recall; importantly it asserts that principled retrieval and integration of both stores resolves partial observability and long-horizon credit assignment bottlenecks.
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: While the paper explains mechanisms for structured memory alone and how it constrains generation, it does not propose, implement, or analyze episodic-memory mechanisms nor their retrieval/integration policies; the combined mechanistic account (structured↔episodic interplay and retrieval into working memory) is absent from the paper.

aggregated_results.paper_title: Deciphering Digital Detectives: Understanding LLM Behaviors and Capabilities in Multi-Agent Mystery Games

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: The paper provides mechanistic descriptions for its modules: MR mitigates context-window limits by storing/retrieving past observations via embeddings, SR decomposes questions to retrieve missing script facts and augment answers, and SV breaks answers into facts and checks them against scripts to reduce hallucination.
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: The submitted theory asserts a distinct causal mechanism in which the combination of explicit structured (graph/relational) memory and episodic (trajectory/observation) memory, with principled retrieval and integration, jointly produces synergistic gains in multi-hop reasoning, navigation/action pruning, and long-horizon credit assignment.
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: The paper does not implement or analyze explicit structured (knowledge-graph/semantic) memory nor provide causal evidence for structured+episodic synergy; therefore the specific mechanistic claim about hybrid structured+episodic memory producing the listed causal benefits is not supported by this work.

aggregated_results.paper_title: MemInsight: Autonomous Memory Augmentation for LLM Agents

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: MemInsight describes a concrete pipeline (attribute mining by perspective/granularity, priority-based annotation, attribute-filtering and embedding-based retrieval) and attributes improved performance to more precise, prioritized memory representations and retrieval.
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: The theory asserts a specific mechanistic decomposition: structured memory provides compact relational representations for multi-hop reasoning, navigation, and action-space pruning, while episodic memory supplies detailed trajectories, procedural recall, and failure/success traces; principled retrieval of both resolves partial observability and long-horizon credit assignment.
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: Those specific causal roles and their interaction (structured→multi-hop/pruning, episodic→credit-assignment/procedural recall) are not articulated, measured, or ablated in the paper, so the proposed mechanism goes beyond the paper's implementation and experiments.

aggregated_results.paper_title: Positive Experience Reflection for Agents in Interactive Text Environments

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: The paper provides a mechanistic explanation for Sweet&Sour: positive reflections reinforce successful strategies, the dual short-/long-term buffers preserve and surface useful reflections across attempts, and the richer context prevents early-success decline ('tilt').
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: The theory statement claims a different mechanism: a synergistic causal interaction between structured relational memory (knowledge/belief graphs) that enables multi-hop reasoning and pruning, and episodic memory that supplies detailed trajectories and procedures; their principled joint retrieval into working memory resolves partial observability and long-horizon credit assignment.
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: The paper contains an episodic/managed-memory mechanistic account but contains no structured-graph memory component nor any analysis of structured+episodic synergy or how their joint retrieval causally improves planning. That structured+episodic mechanistic claim is absent from the paper.

aggregated_results.paper_title: Behavior Cloned Transformers are Neurosymbolic Reasoners

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: The paper offers mechanistic explanations for its results: behavior cloning succeeds because gold trajectories teach the transformer to use module actions and pretrained knowledge aids some tasks, while the DRRN fails due to myopic reward signals and exploration difficulty in large action spaces; it also specifies the operational mechanism of action-injection (module actions become part of A_t and are executed by the module if selected).
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: The theory proposes a distinct mechanistic decomposition: structured memory affords compact relational, multi-hop reasoning and action pruning, while episodic memory supplies detailed procedural recall and successful/failed experience traces, and their principled retrieval together resolves partial observability and long-horizon credit assignment.
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: That particular causal account—how structured and episodic memories interact and why their integration specifically yields robustness, sample efficiency, and generalization—is not derived, tested, or articulated in the paper and therefore represents a new explanatory proposal relative to this work.

aggregated_results.paper_title: Reflexion: Language Agents with Verbal Reinforcement Learning

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: Reflexion supplies an explicit mechanistic account for why episodic verbal memory helps: sparse scalar/environment feedback is converted into actionable natural-language summaries (self-reflections) that are persisted and retrieved to improve credit assignment, error localization, and next-trial planning without weight updates.
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: The theory posits a new causal account in which structured representations provide compact relational state, multi-hop reasoning, and action-space pruning while episodic reflections supply detailed procedural recall, and principled retrieval/integration of both alleviates partial observability and long-horizon credit-assignment.
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: While Reflexion explains the mechanism of episodic verbal reinforcement, it does not propose or test the specific causal interactions or integration protocols between structured relational state representations and episodic reflections; that mechanistic integration is not present in the paper and therefore constitutes a novel mechanistic claim.

aggregated_results.paper_title: Learning Knowledge Graph-based World Models of Textual Environments

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: The paper provides mechanistic explanations for its results: knowledge graphs provide compact relational state representations that mitigate partial observability and combinatorial action spaces, predicting graph differences yields look-ahead, multi-task learning shares information between mapping and acting, and the SOS loss enforces permutation invariance of sets of sequences to improve decoder training.
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: The theory attributes causal roles to two distinct memory modalities: structured memory for multi-hop relational reasoning and action-space pruning, and episodic memory for recall of procedures, trajectories and failed/successful experiences; it further claims their principled integration and retrieval into working memory resolves long-horizon credit assignment and partial observability bottlenecks in LLM agents.
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: That joint, causally complementary mechanisms of structured (KG) plus episodic memory produce the claimed improvements (specifically resolving long-horizon credit assignment and partial observability when integrated into LLM working memory) is not proposed or analyzed in the paper; this explanatory mechanism goes beyond the paper's mechanistic claims about structured memory and multi-task learning.

aggregated_results.paper_title: Language Models are Few-Shot Butlers

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: The paper offers a mechanistic interpretation for its results: pretrained linguistic priors give action generation competence and iterated action modeling augments training data with successful trajectories (treating them as additional demonstrations), effectively combining imitation learning and an RL-style data collection loop under the same action-modeling objective to improve performance and sample efficiency.
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: The theory proposes a different causal mechanism: structured memory provides compact relational representations enabling multi-hop reasoning and action-space pruning, episodic memory supplies detailed traces of past interactions for recall and credit assignment, and principled retrieval that integrates both into working memory yields robustness and generalization.
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: Those specific causal roles for structured representations, their interaction with episodic traces, and the claim that principled cross-memory retrieval is the mechanism of improved long-horizon performance are not proposed, analyzed, or tested in the paper, so the mechanistic account in the theory is novel relative to this work.

aggregated_results.paper_title: Generative Agents: Interactive Simulacra of Human Behavior

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: The paper specifies mechanistic components for long-term coherence: a timestamped natural-language memory stream of observations, an LM-based retrieval scoring function combining recency/importance/relevance (embeddings + LM scoring), periodic reflection synthesis into higher-level abstract memories, and recursive top-down hierarchical planning with just-in-time decomposition and reactive updating; these mechanisms are used to explain why the full architecture outperforms ablations.
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: The Hybrid Memory Synergy Law posits mechanistically distinct roles for two memory modalities: structured semantic/state memory (supporting multi-hop reasoning, navigation grounding, and action-space pruning) and episodic trajectory/reflective memory (supporting detailed recall of events, procedures, and outcomes), and it claims principled joint retrieval of both modalities into working memory resolves partial observability and long-horizon credit assignment bottlenecks.
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: While the paper details mechanisms for episodic natural-language memory, retrieval, reflection, and planning, it does not develop, implement, or evaluate the KG-like structured-memory mechanisms (semantic multi-hop graphs, graph-based pruning or retrieval) nor the claim that their integration resolves long-horizon credit assignment in combinatorial text-game settings; those mechanistic assertions are absent from the paper.

aggregated_results.paper_title: Thespian: Multi-Character Text Role-Playing Game Agents

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: The paper provides a concrete mechanism for the effects it measures: learnable soft character prompts, stacked per-character action logits and value outputs, and a frozen-core attention module that linearly combines raw logits to produce a blended policy for a new character, which explains fast adaptation and avoidance of catastrophic forgetting empirically.
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: The theory proposes a different mechanistic account in which structured memory enables compact relational, multi-hop reasoning and action pruning, episodic memory supplies procedural/outcome recall, and principled retrieval of both into working memory resolves partial observability and long-horizon credit assignment.
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: That specific dual-memory causal mechanism (how KG-like structured state and separate episodic trajectory stores interact via retrieval to produce the claimed benefits) is not implemented, analyzed, or argued in the paper, so the mechanistic explanation is novel relative to this work.

aggregated_results.paper_title: diff History for Neural Language Agents

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: The paper offers mechanistic conjectures for why diff history helps: (1) task-agnostic relational abstraction that localizes salient changes and provides a denser learning signal for finetuning, and (2) token-level 'soft compression' that extends effective context length under fixed LM context budgets, both supported by data (token statistics, ablations).
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: The theory proposes a distinct mechanistic account in which structured memory supplies compact relational, multi-hop reasoning and action-pruning capabilities while episodic memory supplies detailed procedural/experience recall; principled retrieval and integration of both resolve partial observability and long-horizon credit assignment via complementary functions.
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: While the paper explains why a compressed, relational episodic representation (diffs) helps, it does not formalize or empirically test the complementary causal roles, retrieval policies, or integration mechanisms between an explicit structured (graph-like) memory and an episodic trace store. The proposed causal synergy and retrieval-integration dynamics are therefore mechanistically novel beyond the paper's analyses.

aggregated_results.paper_title: Keep CALM and Explore: Language Models for Action Generation in Text-based Games

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: The paper offers a mechanistic explanation for CALM's effectiveness: pretrained LMs capture linguistic priors and human ‘game sense’ which substantially reduce the effective action space, and a DRRN trained on those candidates can learn to select high-value actions via RL; pretraining and diverse human transcripts provide useful inductive biases. It also analyzes operational mechanisms such as the top-k tradeoff and the choice not to fine-tune CALM during RL to avoid overfitting.
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: The Hybrid Memory Synergy Law proposes a different mechanistic account: structured memory gives compact relational representations enabling multi-hop reasoning and action pruning, episodic memory provides detailed procedural recall, and principled retrieval/integration of both resolves partial observability and long-horizon credit assignment.
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: That particular causal/mechanistic decomposition (structured memory for relational multi-hop reasoning + episodic memory for procedural recall + a retrieval/integration protocol producing synergistic benefits) is not developed, tested, or explained in the paper; CALM's mechanism is LM-based candidate generation plus RL re-ranking, not a dual-memory retrieval/integration mechanism.

aggregated_results.paper_title: RAP: Retrieval-Augmented Planning with Contextual Memory for Multimodal LLM Agents

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: The paper provides a concrete mechanistic pipeline: store logs as structured records {task, overall plan, trajectory}, generate retrieval keys from a Reasoner, compute weighted similarity across task/plan/key (using text/image embeddings), retrieve a trajectory window, and use retrieved examples as in‑context prompts to guide action generation by the Executor.
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: The theory gives a mechanistic account attributing complementary causal roles to two memory types: structured memory supplies compact relational, multi-hop reasoning and action-space pruning, while episodic memory supplies detailed procedural recall; their principled joint retrieval purportedly resolves partial observability and long-horizon credit assignment.
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: The paper's mechanistic account covers episodic retrieval → in-context guidance but does not implement or validate the claimed causal roles of an explicit structured relational memory (multi-hop pruning, relational reasoning) nor demonstrate that combining the two memories resolves long‑horizon credit assignment in the way the theory asserts.

aggregated_results.paper_title: Pre-trained Language Models as Prior Knowledge for Playing Text-based Games

dimension_evaluation.what_is_known: The paper offers a mechanistic explanation for its LM results: pre-trained LMs supply linguistic/world/game priors that produce richer observation encodings, make reward cues more accessible, reduce reliance on learning language from scratch, and thereby improve action selection and some exploration; fine-tuning on gameplay transcripts adapts these priors to the domain.
dimension_evaluation.what_introduced: The theory proposes a different mechanistic decomposition: structured memory provides compact relational, multi-hop reasoning and action-space pruning, episodic memory provides recall of detailed procedures and past successes/failures aiding long-horizon credit assignment, and their principled retrieval/integration into working memory is the causal mechanism producing robustness, sample efficiency, and generalization.
dimension_evaluation.what_novel: That dual-memory causal account (distinct complementary roles for structured vs episodic memory plus a retrieval/integration policy) is not proposed, derived, or evaluated in the paper; the paper's mechanistic story concerns LM priors rather than an explicit structured↔episodic retrieval-mediated mechanism.

conceptual_reframing_abstraction.what_is_known: The field exhibits substantial prior work on memory components for LLM text-game agents: one paper (AriGraph) explicitly formalizes a hybrid graph+episodic memory architecture with integrated retrieval pipelines as its core contribution; comprehensive surveys catalog memory abstractions (working/long-term, chunks/KV/trees/graphs/parametric) and operations (consolidation, structuring, retrieval); taxonomies organize knowledge types (structured affordance vs episodic action-memory) and injection strategies; and multiple papers implement either structured state representations (knowledge graphs, belief graphs) or episodic memory components (reflections, trajectory buffers) as individual design choices without formalizing their complementary integration.
conceptual_reframing_abstraction.what_introduced: The theory elevates the purposeful integration of hybrid structured (relational/semantic) and episodic (trajectory/reflective) memory with principled retrieval into working memory as the canonical, general organizing abstraction for robust LLM agent design in text games. It reframes this not as a collection of independent techniques or a specialized solution but as a fundamental architectural law prescribing synergistic combination of relational semantic state and trajectory/reflective experience as the core design principle for overcoming partial observability and long-horizon credit assignment across diverse text-game environments.
conceptual_reframing_abstraction.what_novel: The conceptual reframing exhibits a clear gradient: it is the explicit core organizing framework in one specialized paper (AriGraph, 1/23); it appears as peripheral/cataloged content in surveys and taxonomies that enumerate but don't center this integration (3/23); it is derivable but unstated from papers implementing structured or episodic components separately without formalizing their complementary synergistic roles (6/23); and it is genuinely novel—representing an entirely different organizing principle—relative to the majority of the field (13/23) focused on neurosymbolic reasoning, action generation, ToM planning, transfer learning, and other frameworks. Overall, while explicitly instantiated in isolated specialized work, the abstraction remains unstated as a general field-wide design principle and is derivable from but not recognized in the collective body of component-focused research.
